Benchmark Results for gemini-3.1-pro-preview

Overall Summary

Test 1: Travelling Salesman Problem Solver (C++) Score: 5.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY number of cities from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 10-40 cities (brute-force feasible for smallest cases)
- **Medium**: 100-1000 cities (requires heuristics)
- **Large**: 5000-100000 cities (requires efficient heuristics)
- **Extreme**: 500000-5000000 cities (requires very fast approximations)
- **Epic**: 10000000+ cities (requires highly optimized approximations)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with city counts ranging from 10 to 10000000. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of cities)
Lines 2..N+1: x y coordinates (floats)

**Output format (stdout):**
One line with N integers: the visit order of city indices (0-indexed), starting at city 0.
Do NOT include returning to city 0 (return is implicit).

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for 1000,000,000 cities
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable routes at all scales
4. **Correctness**: Output must be a valid permutation of 0..N-1 starting with 0

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function that reads stdin and writes stdout.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on N.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: 10 Cities
View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of cities, find the shortest round-trip route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. This is one of the most famous hard problems in computer science — checking every possible route is only feasible for a handful of cities, so the AI must use clever shortcuts to find a good route quickly.

Subpasses scale from 10 cities up to 1 000, so brute-force is impossible for the larger cases. The AI's route length is compared against a simple baseline; beating it earns full marks.

 1.0000
[10 cities] Route distance: 2449.07, Baseline: 2922.93, Ratio: 0.84x, Time: 0.0s - excellent (within 10% of baseline)

TSP Solver - 10 Cities

View Code (8631 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <cstdint>
#include <numeric>

using namespace std;

#pragma optimize("t", on)

struct Point {
    double x, y;
};

inline double dist_sq(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    double dx = a.x - b.x;
    double dy = a.y - b.y;
    return dx*dx + dy*dy;
}

inline double dist(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    return std::sqrt(dist_sq(a, b));
}

double compute_tour_length(const vector<int>& tour, const vector<Point>& pts) {
    double d = 0;
    int n = tour.size();
    for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) {
        d += dist(pts[tour[i]], pts[tour[i+1]]);
    }
    d += dist(pts[tour[n-1]], pts[tour[0]]);
    return d;
}

void print_tour(const vector<int>& tour, int N) {
    int start_idx = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        if (tour[i] == 0) {
            start_idx = i;
            break;
        }
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        cout << tour[(start_idx + i) % N] << (i == N - 1 ? "" : " ");
    }
    cout << "\n";
}

void solve_trivial(int N, const vector<Point>& pts) {
    vector<int> p(N);
    iota(p.begin(), p.end(), 0);
    double best_dist = 1e38;
    vector<int> best_p = p;
    
    do {
        double d = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < N - 1; i++) d += dist(pts[p[i]], pts[p[i+1]]);
        d += dist(pts[p[N-1]], pts[p[0]]);
        if (d < best_dist) {
            best_dist = d;
            best_p = p;
        }
    } while (next_permutation(p.begin() + 1, p.end()));
    
    print_tour(best_p, N);
}

void apply_2opt(vector<int>& tour, int N, const vector<Point>& pts, int max_passes = 20) {
    bool improved = true;
    int passes = 0;
    
    vector<double> edge_len(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        edge_len[i] = dist(pts[tour[i]], pts[tour[(i+1)%N]]);
    }
    
    while (improved && passes < max_passes) {
        improved = false;
        passes++;
        for (int i = 0; i < N - 1; i++) {
            for (int j = i + 2; j < N; j++) {
                if (i == 0 && j == N - 1) continue;
                
                double d1 = edge_len[i] + edge_len[j];
                int p1 = tour[i], p2 = tour[i+1];
                int p3 = tour[j], p4 = tour[(j+1)%N];
                
                double a = dist_sq(pts[p1], pts[p3]);
                double b = dist_sq(pts[p2], pts[p4]);
                
                if (a + b < d1 * d1 + 1e-7) {
                    double sqa = std::sqrt(a);
                    double sqb = std::sqrt(b);
                    double d2 = sqa + sqb;
                    if (d2 < d1 - 1e-9) {
                        int left = i + 1, right = j;
                        while (left < right) {
                            swap(tour[left], tour[right]);
                            left++; right--;
                        }
                        
                        edge_len[i] = sqa;
                        edge_len[j] = sqb;
                        
                        int left_e = i + 1, right_e = j - 1;
                        while (left_e < right_e) {
                            swap(edge_len[left_e], edge_len[right_e]);
                            left_e++; right_e--;
                        }
                        
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

vector<int> build_nn(int start_node, int N, const vector<Point>& pts) {
    vector<int> tour;
    tour.reserve(N);
    vector<bool> visited(N, false);
    tour.push_back(start_node);
    visited[start_node] = true;
    int curr = start_node;
    for (int step = 1; step < N; step++) {
        double min_d2 = 1e38;
        int next_node = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
            if (!visited[i]) {
                double d2 = dist_sq(pts[curr], pts[i]);
                if (d2 < min_d2) {
                    min_d2 = d2;
                    next_node = i;
                }
            }
        }
        tour.push_back(next_node);
        visited[next_node] = true;
        curr = next_node;
    }
    return tour;
}

void solve_small(int N, const vector<Point>& pts) {
    int K = min(N, 100);
    double best_dist = 1e38;
    vector<int> best_tour;
    
    for (int i = 0; i < K; i++) {
        vector<int> tour = build_nn(i, N, pts);
        apply_2opt(tour, N, pts, 20);
        double d = compute_tour_length(tour, pts);
        if (d < best_dist) {
            best_dist = d;
            best_tour = std::move(tour);
        }
    }
    print_tour(best_tour, N);
}

void solve_medium(int N, const vector<Point>& pts) {
    vector<int> tour = build_nn(0, N, pts);
    int max_passes = (N > 5000) ? 5 : 20;
    apply_2opt(tour, N, pts, max_passes);
    print_tour(tour, N);
}

uint64_t hilbert_index(uint32_t x, uint32_t y) {
    uint64_t d = 0;
    for (int i = 31; i >= 0; i--) {
        uint32_t rx = (x >> i) & 1;
        uint32_t ry = (y >> i) & 1;
        d = (d << 2) | ((3 * rx) ^ ry);
        if (ry == 0) {
            if (rx == 1) {
                x = ~x;
                y = ~y;
            }
            uint32_t t = x;
            x = y;
            y = t;
        }
    }
    return d;
}

void solve_large(int N, const vector<Point>& pts) {
    double min_x = 1e38, max_x = -1e38;
    double min_y = 1e38, max_y = -1e38;
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        if (pts[i].x < min_x) min_x = pts[i].x;
        if (pts[i].x > max_x) max_x = pts[i].x;
        if (pts[i].y < min_y) min_y = pts[i].y;
        if (pts[i].y > max_y) max_y = pts[i].y;
    }
    
    double range_x = max_x - min_x;
    double range_y = max_y - min_y;
    double max_range = max(range_x, range_y);
    if (max_range == 0.0) max_range = 1.0;
    
    double scale = 4294967295.0 / max_range;
    
    vector<pair<uint64_t, int>> hilbert(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        uint32_t nx = min((uint64_t)4294967295ULL, (uint64_t)((pts[i].x - min_x) * scale));
        uint32_t ny = min((uint64_t)4294967295ULL, (uint64_t)((pts[i].y - min_y) * scale));
        hilbert[i] = {hilbert_index(nx, ny), i};
    }
    
    sort(hilbert.begin(), hilbert.end());
    
    vector<int> tour(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        tour[i] = hilbert[i].second;
    }
    
    int window = 50;
    if (N > 1000000) window = 20;
    if (N > 5000000) window = 10;
    
    vector<double> edge_len(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        edge_len[i] = dist(pts[tour[i]], pts[tour[(i+1)%N]]);
    }
    
    bool improved = true;
    int passes = 0;
    int max_passes = 10;
    
    while (improved && passes < max_passes) {
        improved = false;
        passes++;
        for (int i = 0; i < N - 1; i++) {
            int max_j = min(N, i + window);
            for (int j = i + 2; j < max_j; j++) {
                if (i == 0 && j == N - 1) continue;
                
                double d1 = edge_len[i] + edge_len[j];
                int p1 = tour[i], p2 = tour[i+1];
                int p3 = tour[j], p4 = tour[(j+1)%N];
                
                double a = dist_sq(pts[p1], pts[p3]);
                double b = dist_sq(pts[p2], pts[p4]);
                
                if (a + b < d1 * d1 + 1e-7) {
                    double sqa = std::sqrt(a);
                    double sqb = std::sqrt(b);
                    double d2 = sqa + sqb;
                    if (d2 < d1 - 1e-9) {
                        int left = i + 1, right = j;
                        while (left < right) {
                            swap(tour[left], tour[right]);
                            left++; right--;
                        }
                        
                        edge_len[i] = sqa;
                        edge_len[j] = sqb;
                        
                        int left_e = i + 1, right_e = j - 1;
                        while (left_e < right_e) {
                            swap(edge_len[left_e], edge_len[right_e]);
                            left_e++; right_e--;
                        }
                        
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    print_tour(tour, N);
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(NULL);
    
    int N;
    if (!(cin >> N)) return 0;
    
    vector<Point> pts(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        cin >> pts[i].x >> pts[i].y;
    }
    
    if (N <= 11) {
        solve_trivial(N, pts);
    } else if (N <= 1000) {
        solve_small(N, pts);
    } else if (N <= 30000) {
        solve_medium(N, pts);
    } else {
        solve_large(N, pts);
    }
    
    return 0;
}

Test 2: Chinese Postman Problem Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Chinese Postman Problem (Route Inspection Problem) in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-20 nodes, 8-45 edges (small graphs, exact algorithms feasible)
- **Medium**: 35-200 nodes, 80-600 edges (requires efficient algorithms)
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, 2000-5000 edges (requires optimized implementations)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, 30000-80000 edges (requires very fast algorithms)
- **Epic**: 50000-100000 nodes, 500000-1000000 edges (requires highly optimized algorithms)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 100000 nodes. The same function must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v w (edge endpoints, 0-indexed, weight integer)

**Output format (stdout):**
A sequence of node indices (whitespace-separated) representing a route that starts at node 0,
traverses EVERY edge at least once, and returns to node 0.
You may optionally prefix with a single integer L (route length). If present, it must match the number of nodes listed.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Correctness**: Must traverse every edge at least once

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt

View chain of thought

Imagine a mail carrier who must walk along every street in a neighbourhood and return home. Some streets may need to be walked twice if the network doesn't allow a perfect loop — the goal is to minimise that extra walking.

The AI must figure out which streets (edges) to re-traverse and produce a complete route. Subpasses increase the graph size, making the problem harder to solve efficiently.

Chinese Postman Problem - 6 nodes, 8 edges

View Code (12068 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;

public class ChinesePostman
{
    class Scanner
    {
        Stream stream;
        byte[] buffer = new byte[1024 * 64];
        int head, tail;

        public Scanner(Stream s)
        {
            stream = s;
        }

        public int ReadInt()
        {
            int c = Read();
            while (c <= 32)
            {
                if (c == -1) return -1;
                c = Read();
            }
            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = Read();
            }
            int res = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
                    res = res * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = Read();
            }
            return res * sign;
        }

        int Read()
        {
            if (head >= tail)
            {
                head = 0;
                tail = stream.Read(buffer, 0, buffer.Length);
                if (tail == 0) return -1;
            }
            return buffer[head++];
        }
    }

    class Edge
    {
        public int to;
        public int id;
        public int weight;
        public Edge(int to, int id, int weight)
        {
            this.to = to;
            this.id = id;
            this.weight = weight;
        }
    }

    static List<Edge>[] adj;
    static List<bool> edgeUsed;
    static int edgeCount = 0;

    static void AddDuplicateEdge(int u, int v, int w)
    {
        adj[u].Add(new Edge(v, edgeCount, w));
        adj[v].Add(new Edge(u, edgeCount, w));
        edgeUsed.Add(false);
        edgeCount++;
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        Scanner scanner = new Scanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int N = scanner.ReadInt();
        if (N == -1) return;
        int M = scanner.ReadInt();

        adj = new List<Edge>[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) adj[i] = new List<Edge>();
        edgeUsed = new List<bool>(M + 10000);

        for (int i = 0; i < M; i++)
        {
            int u = scanner.ReadInt();
            int v = scanner.ReadInt();
            int w = scanner.ReadInt();
            adj[u].Add(new Edge(v, edgeCount, w));
            adj[v].Add(new Edge(u, edgeCount, w));
            edgeUsed.Add(false);
            edgeCount++;
        }

        List<int> oddNodes = new List<int>();
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            if (adj[i].Count % 2 != 0)
            {
                oddNodes.Add(i);
            }
        }

        int K = oddNodes.Count;

        if (K > 0)
        {
            if (K <= 20)
            {
                SolveExactDP(N, oddNodes);
            }
            else if (K <= 400)
            {
                SolveGreedy2Opt(N, oddNodes);
            }
            else
            {
                SolveMSDP(N, oddNodes);
            }
        }

        List<int> circuit = new List<int>();
        Stack<int> currPath = new Stack<int>();
        int[] edgeIndex = new int[N];

        currPath.Push(0);
        while (currPath.Count > 0)
        {
            int u = currPath.Peek();
            bool found = false;
            while (edgeIndex[u] < adj[u].Count)
            {
                Edge e = adj[u][edgeIndex[u]];
                edgeIndex[u]++;
                if (!edgeUsed[e.id])
                {
                    edgeUsed[e.id] = true;
                    currPath.Push(e.to);
                    found = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (!found)
            {
                circuit.Add(currPath.Pop());
            }
        }

        circuit.Reverse();

        using (StreamWriter writer = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), bufferSize: 65536))
        {
            writer.Write(circuit.Count);
            for (int i = 0; i < circuit.Count; i++)
            {
                writer.Write(" ");
                writer.Write(circuit[i]);
            }
            writer.WriteLine();
        }
    }

    static void SolveExactDP(int N, List<int> oddNodes)
    {
        int K = oddNodes.Count;
        long[][] distMat = new long[K][];
        int[][] parentMat = new int[K][];

        for (int i = 0; i < K; i++)
        {
            distMat[i] = new long[K];
            parentMat[i] = new int[N];
            long[] d = new long[N];
            Array.Fill(d, long.MaxValue);
            d[oddNodes[i]] = 0;
            PriorityQueue<int, long> pq = new PriorityQueue<int, long>();
            pq.Enqueue(oddNodes[i], 0);

            while (pq.Count > 0)
            {
                pq.TryDequeue(out int curr, out long curD);
                if (curD > d[curr]) continue;

                foreach (var e in adj[curr])
                {
                    long nd = curD + e.weight;
                    if (nd < d[e.to])
                    {
                        d[e.to] = nd;
                        parentMat[i][e.to] = curr;
                        pq.Enqueue(e.to, nd);
                    }
                }
            }

            for (int j = 0; j < K; j++)
            {
                distMat[i][j] = d[oddNodes[j]];
            }
        }

        long[] dp = new long[1 << K];
        Array.Fill(dp, long.MaxValue);
        dp[0] = 0;
        int[] bestU = new int[1 << K];
        int[] bestV = new int[1 << K];
        int[] prevMask = new int[1 << K];

        for (int mask = 0; mask < (1 << K); mask++)
        {
            if (dp[mask] == long.MaxValue) continue;
            int u = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < K; i++)
            {
                if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) { u = i; break; }
            }
            if (u == -1) continue;

            for (int v = u + 1; v < K; v++)
            {
                if ((mask & (1 << v)) == 0)
                {
                    int nmask = mask | (1 << u) | (1 << v);
                    long ndist = dp[mask] + distMat[u][v];
                    if (ndist < dp[nmask])
                    {
                        dp[nmask] = ndist;
                        prevMask[nmask] = mask;
                        bestU[nmask] = u;
                        bestV[nmask] = v;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        int currMask = (1 << K) - 1;
        while (currMask > 0)
        {
            int u = bestU[currMask];
            int v = bestV[currMask];

            int currNode = oddNodes[v];
            while (currNode != oddNodes[u])
            {
                int p = parentMat[u][currNode];
                AddDuplicateEdge(p, currNode, 0);
                currNode = p;
            }

            currMask = prevMask[currMask];
        }
    }

    static void SolveGreedy2Opt(int N, List<int> oddNodes)
    {
        int K = oddNodes.Count;
        long[][] distMat = new long[K][];
        int[][] parentMat = new int[K][];

        for (int i = 0; i < K; i++)
        {
            distMat[i] = new long[K];
            parentMat[i] = new int[N];
            long[] d = new long[N];
            Array.Fill(d, long.MaxValue);
            d[oddNodes[i]] = 0;
            PriorityQueue<int, long> pq = new PriorityQueue<int, long>();
            pq.Enqueue(oddNodes[i], 0);

            while (pq.Count > 0)
            {
                pq.TryDequeue(out int curr, out long curD);
                if (curD > d[curr]) continue;

                foreach (var e in adj[curr])
                {
                    long nd = curD + e.weight;
                    if (nd < d[e.to])
                    {
                        d[e.to] = nd;
                        parentMat[i][e.to] = curr;
                        pq.Enqueue(e.to, nd);
                    }
                }
            }

            for (int j = 0; j < K; j++)
            {
                distMat[i][j] = d[oddNodes[j]];
            }
        }

        List<Tuple<long, int, int>> allPairs = new List<Tuple<long, int, int>>();
        for (int i = 0; i < K; i++)
        {
            for (int j = i + 1; j < K; j++)
            {
                allPairs.Add(new Tuple<long, int, int>(distMat[i][j], i, j));
            }
        }
        allPairs.Sort();

        bool[] paired = new bool[K];
        List<Tuple<int, int>> pairs = new List<Tuple<int, int>>();

        foreach (var p in allPairs)
        {
            int u = p.Item2;
            int v = p.Item3;
            if (!paired[u] && !paired[v])
            {
                paired[u] = true;
                paired[v] = true;
                pairs.Add(new Tuple<int, int>(u, v));
            }
        }

        bool improved = true;
        while (improved)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < pairs.Count; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j < pairs.Count; j++)
                {
                    int A = pairs[i].Item1, B = pairs[i].Item2;
                    int C = pairs[j].Item1, D = pairs[j].Item2;

                    long currentDist = distMat[A][B] + distMat[C][D];
                    long d1 = distMat[A][C] + distMat[B][D];
                    long d2 = distMat[A][D] + distMat[B][C];

                    if (d1 < currentDist && d1 <= d2)
                    {
                        pairs[i] = new Tuple<int, int>(A, C);
                        pairs[j] = new Tuple<int, int>(B, D);
                        improved = true;
                    }
                    else if (d2 < currentDist && d2 < d1)
                    {
                        pairs[i] = new Tuple<int, int>(A, D);
                        pairs[j] = new Tuple<int, int>(B, C);
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        foreach (var p in pairs)
        {
            int u = p.Item1;
            int v = p.Item2;
            int currNode = oddNodes[v];
            while (currNode != oddNodes[u])
            {
                int pr = parentMat[u][currNode];
                AddDuplicateEdge(pr, currNode, 0);
                currNode = pr;
            }
        }
    }

    static void SolveMSDP(int N, List<int> oddNodes)
    {
        long[] dist = new long[N];
        int[] owner = new int[N];
        int[] parent = new int[N];
        Array.Fill(dist, long.MaxValue);
        Array.Fill(owner, -1);
        Array.Fill(parent, -1);

        PriorityQueue<int, long> pq = new PriorityQueue<int, long>();
        bool[] isPaired = new bool[N];

        foreach (int u in oddNodes)
        {
            dist[u] = 0;
            owner[u] = u;
            pq.Enqueue(u, 0);
        }

        while (pq.Count > 0)
        {
            pq.TryDequeue(out int curr, out long curD);

            if (curD > dist[curr]) continue;

            int O1 = owner[curr];
            if (isPaired[O1]) continue;

            foreach (var e in adj[curr])
            {
                int O2 = owner[e.to];

                if (O2 != -1 && O2 != O1 && !isPaired[O2])
                {
                    isPaired[O1] = true;
                    isPaired[O2] = true;

                    int temp = curr;
                    while (temp != O1)
                    {
                        int p = parent[temp];
                        AddDuplicateEdge(p, temp, 0);
                        temp = p;
                    }

                    temp = e.to;
                    while (temp != O2)
                    {
                        int p = parent[temp];
                        AddDuplicateEdge(p, temp, 0);
                        temp = p;
                    }

                    AddDuplicateEdge(curr, e.to, 0);
                    break;
                }

                long nd = curD + e.weight;
                if (O2 == -1 || isPaired[O2] || nd < dist[e.to])
                {
                    dist[e.to] = nd;
                    owner[e.to] = O1;
                    parent[e.to] = curr;
                    pq.Enqueue(e.to, nd);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

Test 3: Graph Layout - Edge Crossing Minimization (Rust) Score: 4.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Graph Layout problem (Edge Crossing Minimization) in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-15 nodes, small trees and cycles (planar, 0 crossings achievable)
- **Medium**: 20-100 nodes, dense planar and non-planar graphs
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, random dense graphs (many crossings unavoidable)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, very large random graphs

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 200,000 nodes. The same solver must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v (undirected edge endpoints, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
Output N lines, one per node, with: x y coordinates (floats).
You may optionally prefix with a single integer N on the first line.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable layouts with minimal crossings
4. **Robustness**: Handle disconnected components, self-loops, and parallel edges gracefully

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a graph (a set of dots connected by lines), arrange the dots on a 2D canvas so that as few lines cross each other as possible. A clean, readable layout is the goal — tangled spaghetti is the enemy.

Minimising crossings is extremely hard in general. The AI must place nodes intelligently; the baseline simply puts them in a circle. Subpasses increase the number of nodes and edges.

 1.0000
[tree, 6 nodes, 5 edges] Crossings: 0, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.9s - perfect (0 crossings)

Graph Layout - tree (6 nodes, 5 edges)

View Code (13618 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read};
use std::collections::{VecDeque, HashMap};
use std::time::Instant;
use std::thread;

struct QuadTree {
    nodes: Vec<QuadNode>,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct QuadNode {
    mass: f32,
    cm_x: f32,
    cm_y: f32,
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    half_w: f32,
    first_child: u32,
    node_id: u32,
}

impl QuadNode {
    fn new_empty(x: f32, y: f32, half_w: f32) -> Self {
        Self {
            mass: 0.0,
            cm_x: 0.0,
            cm_y: 0.0,
            x,
            y,
            half_w,
            first_child: !0,
            node_id: !0,
        }
    }
}

fn get_quad(px: f32, py: f32, cx: f32, cy: f32) -> u32 {
    let mut q = 0;
    if px >= cx { q |= 1; }
    if py >= cy { q |= 2; }
    q
}

impl QuadTree {
    fn new(min_x: f32, min_y: f32, max_x: f32, max_y: f32, n: usize) -> Self {
        let center_x = (min_x + max_x) / 2.0;
        let center_y = (min_y + max_y) / 2.0;
        let mut half_w = (max_x - min_x).max(max_y - min_y) / 2.0;
        if half_w < 1e-4 { half_w = 1.0; }
        
        let mut nodes = Vec::with_capacity(n * 4 + 16);
        nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(center_x, center_y, half_w));
        Self { nodes }
    }

    fn insert(&mut self, pt_idx: usize, pos_x: &[f32], pos_y: &[f32]) {
        let p_x = pos_x[pt_idx];
        let p_y = pos_y[pt_idx];
        let mut curr = 0;
        
        loop {
            {
                let node = &mut self.nodes[curr as usize];
                let new_mass = node.mass + 1.0;
                node.cm_x = (node.cm_x * node.mass + p_x) / new_mass;
                node.cm_y = (node.cm_y * node.mass + p_y) / new_mass;
                node.mass = new_mass;
            }

            let first_child = self.nodes[curr as usize].first_child;
            let node_id = self.nodes[curr as usize].node_id;
            let node_x = self.nodes[curr as usize].x;
            let node_y = self.nodes[curr as usize].y;
            let node_hw = self.nodes[curr as usize].half_w;

            if first_child != !0 {
                let quad = get_quad(p_x, p_y, node_x, node_y);
                curr = first_child + quad;
            } else if node_id != !0 {
                if node_hw < 1e-4 {
                    break;
                }
                
                let exist_idx = node_id as usize;
                let ex_x = pos_x[exist_idx];
                let ex_y = pos_y[exist_idx];
                
                self.nodes[curr as usize].node_id = !0;
                let new_first_child = self.nodes.len() as u32;
                self.nodes[curr as usize].first_child = new_first_child;
                
                let nh = node_hw / 2.0;
                
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x - nh, node_y - nh, nh));
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x + nh, node_y - nh, nh));
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x - nh, node_y + nh, nh));
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x + nh, node_y + nh, nh));
                
                let ex_quad = get_quad(ex_x, ex_y, node_x, node_y);
                self.insert_existing(new_first_child + ex_quad, exist_idx, ex_x, ex_y, pos_x, pos_y);
                
                let p_quad = get_quad(p_x, p_y, node_x, node_y);
                curr = new_first_child + p_quad;
            } else {
                self.nodes[curr as usize].node_id = pt_idx as u32;
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    fn insert_existing(&mut self, mut curr: u32, pt_idx: usize, p_x: f32, p_y: f32, pos_x: &[f32], pos_y: &[f32]) {
        loop {
            {
                let node = &mut self.nodes[curr as usize];
                let new_mass = node.mass + 1.0;
                node.cm_x = (node.cm_x * node.mass + p_x) / new_mass;
                node.cm_y = (node.cm_y * node.mass + p_y) / new_mass;
                node.mass = new_mass;
            }
            
            let first_child = self.nodes[curr as usize].first_child;
            let node_id = self.nodes[curr as usize].node_id;
            let node_x = self.nodes[curr as usize].x;
            let node_y = self.nodes[curr as usize].y;
            let node_hw = self.nodes[curr as usize].half_w;
            
            if first_child != !0 {
                let quad = get_quad(p_x, p_y, node_x, node_y);
                curr = first_child + quad;
            } else if node_id != !0 {
                if node_hw < 1e-4 { break; }
                let exist_idx = node_id as usize;
                let ex_x = pos_x[exist_idx];
                let ex_y = pos_y[exist_idx];
                
                self.nodes[curr as usize].node_id = !0;
                let new_first_child = self.nodes.len() as u32;
                self.nodes[curr as usize].first_child = new_first_child;
                
                let nh = node_hw / 2.0;
                
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x - nh, node_y - nh, nh));
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x + nh, node_y - nh, nh));
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x - nh, node_y + nh, nh));
                self.nodes.push(QuadNode::new_empty(node_x + nh, node_y + nh, nh));
                
                let ex_quad = get_quad(ex_x, ex_y, node_x, node_y);
                self.insert_existing(new_first_child + ex_quad, exist_idx, ex_x, ex_y, pos_x, pos_y);
                
                let p_quad = get_quad(p_x, p_y, node_x, node_y);
                curr = new_first_child + p_quad;
            } else {
                self.nodes[curr as usize].node_id = pt_idx as u32;
                break;
            }
        }
    }
}

fn compute_repulsion(tree: &QuadTree, p_idx: usize, p_x: f32, p_y: f32, k2: f32, theta: f32) -> (f32, f32) {
    let mut stack = Vec::with_capacity(128);
    stack.push(0u32);
    
    let mut fx = 0.0;
    let mut fy = 0.0;
    
    while let Some(curr) = stack.pop() {
        let node = &tree.nodes[curr as usize];
        if node.mass == 0.0 { continue; }
        
        let width = node.half_w * 2.0;
        
        if node.first_child == !0 {
            if node.node_id == p_idx as u32 {
                continue;
            }
        }
        
        let mut dx = node.cm_x - p_x;
        let mut dy = node.cm_y - p_y;
        
        if dx.abs() < 1e-5 && dy.abs() < 1e-5 {
            dx = if p_idx % 2 == 0 { 1e-4 } else { -1e-4 };
            dy = if p_idx % 3 == 0 { 1e-4 } else { -1e-4 };
        }
        
        let mut dist_sq = dx * dx + dy * dy;
        if dist_sq < 1e-4 { dist_sq = 1e-4; }
        let dist = dist_sq.sqrt();
        
        if node.first_child == !0 || width / dist < theta {
            let f = (k2 / dist) * node.mass;
            fx -= f * (dx / dist);
            fy -= f * (dy / dist);
        } else {
            stack.push(node.first_child);
            stack.push(node.first_child + 1);
            stack.push(node.first_child + 2);
            stack.push(node.first_child + 3);
        }
    }
    (fx, fy)
}

fn xorshift(seed: &mut u64) -> f32 {
    *seed ^= *seed << 13;
    *seed ^= *seed >> 7;
    *seed ^= *seed << 17;
    ((*seed as u32) as f32) / (std::u32::MAX as f32)
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = Vec::new();
    if io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input).is_err() {
        return;
    }
    let input_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&input);
    let mut tokens = input_str.split_whitespace();
    
    let n: usize = match tokens.next() {
        Some(s) => match s.parse() {
            Ok(val) => val,
            Err(_) => return,
        },
        None => return,
    };
    
    let m: usize = match tokens.next() {
        Some(s) => match s.parse() {
            Ok(val) => val,
            Err(_) => return,
        },
        None => return,
    };
    
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(m);
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u: usize = match tokens.next() {
            Some(s) => s.parse().unwrap_or(0),
            None => break,
        };
        let v: usize = match tokens.next() {
            Some(s) => s.parse().unwrap_or(0),
            None => break,
        };
        edges.push((u, v));
    }
    
    if n == 0 { return; }
    if n == 1 {
        println!("0.0 0.0");
        return;
    }

    let mut pos_x = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut pos_y = vec![0.0; n];
    
    let mut adj = vec![vec![]; n];
    for &(u, v) in &edges {
        if u < n && v < n {
            adj[u].push(v);
            adj[v].push(u);
        }
    }
    
    let mut visited = vec![false; n];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    let mut rng_seed = 123456789u64;

    for i in 0..n {
        if !visited[i] {
            let cx = (xorshift(&mut rng_seed) - 0.5) * (n as f32).sqrt() * 2.0;
            let cy = (xorshift(&mut rng_seed) - 0.5) * (n as f32).sqrt() * 2.0;
            
            q.push_back((i, 0));
            visited[i] = true;
            
            let mut level_counts = HashMap::new();
            let mut node_levels = Vec::new();
            
            while let Some((u, d)) = q.pop_front() {
                node_levels.push((u, d));
                *level_counts.entry(d).or_insert(0) += 1;
                
                for &v in &adj[u] {
                    if !visited[v] {
                        visited[v] = true;
                        q.push_back((v, d + 1));
                    }
                }
            }
            
            let mut level_idx = HashMap::new();
            for (u, d) in node_levels {
                let idx = level_idx.entry(d).or_insert(0);
                let count = level_counts[&d];
                
                if d == 0 {
                    pos_x[u] = cx;
                    pos_y[u] = cy;
                } else {
                    let radius = (d as f32).max(1.0) * 2.0 + (count as f32).sqrt() * 1.0;
                    let angle = 2.0 * std::f32::consts::PI * (*idx as f32) / (count as f32) + (d as f32 * 0.1);
                    pos_x[u] = cx + angle.cos() * radius + (xorshift(&mut rng_seed) - 0.5) * 0.1;
                    pos_y[u] = cy + angle.sin() * radius + (xorshift(&mut rng_seed) - 0.5) * 0.1;
                }
                *idx += 1;
            }
        }
    }
    
    let start_time = Instant::now();
    let time_limit = 28.0;
    let max_iters = 1500;
    let mut iter = 0;
    
    let mut disp_x = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut disp_y = vec![0.0; n];
    
    let num_threads = std::thread::available_parallelism().map(|p| p.get()).unwrap_or(1);
    let chunk_size = (n + num_threads - 1) / num_threads;

    while iter < max_iters {
        let elapsed = start_time.elapsed().as_secs_f32();
        if elapsed > time_limit {
            break;
        }
        
        let progress = (elapsed / time_limit).max(iter as f32 / max_iters as f32).min(1.0);
        let t0 = 5.0 + (n as f32).sqrt() * 0.05;
        let temp = t0 * (1.0 - progress) + 0.05;
        
        let mut min_x = pos_x[0]; let mut max_x = pos_x[0];
        let mut min_y = pos_y[0]; let mut max_y = pos_y[0];
        for i in 1..n {
            if pos_x[i] < min_x { min_x = pos_x[i]; }
            if pos_x[i] > max_x { max_x = pos_x[i]; }
            if pos_y[i] < min_y { min_y = pos_y[i]; }
            if pos_y[i] > max_y { max_y = pos_y[i]; }
        }
        if max_x - min_x < 1e-4 { max_x += 1.0; min_x -= 1.0; }
        if max_y - min_y < 1e-4 { max_y += 1.0; min_y -= 1.0; }
        
        let mut tree = QuadTree::new(min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y, n);
        for i in 0..n {
            tree.insert(i, &pos_x, &pos_y);
        }
        
        thread::scope(|s| {
            let mut dx_chunks = disp_x.chunks_mut(chunk_size);
            let mut dy_chunks = disp_y.chunks_mut(chunk_size);
            
            for (i, (dx_chunk, dy_chunk)) in dx_chunks.zip(dy_chunks).enumerate() {
                let start_idx = i * chunk_size;
                let tree_ref = &tree;
                let px_ref = &pos_x;
                let py_ref = &pos_y;
                
                s.spawn(move || {
                    for j in 0..dx_chunk.len() {
                        let node_idx = start_idx + j;
                        let (rx, ry) = compute_repulsion(tree_ref, node_idx, px_ref[node_idx], py_ref[node_idx], 1.0, 0.8);
                        dx_chunk[j] = rx;
                        dy_chunk[j] = ry;
                    }
                });
            }
        });
        
        for &(u, v) in &edges {
            if u >= n || v >= n { continue; }
            let dx = pos_x[v] - pos_x[u];
            let dy = pos_y[v] - pos_y[u];
            let mut dist_sq = dx * dx + dy * dy;
            if dist_sq < 1e-4 { dist_sq = 1e-4; }
            let dist = dist_sq.sqrt();
            
            let f = dist_sq; 
            let fx = f * (dx / dist);
            let fy = f * (dy / dist);
            
            disp_x[u] += fx;
            disp_y[u] += fy;
            disp_x[v] -= fx;
            disp_y[v] -= fy;
        }
        
        for i in 0..n {
            let mut dx = disp_x[i];
            let mut dy = disp_y[i];
            
            dx -= pos_x[i] * 0.01;
            dy -= pos_y[i] * 0.01;
            
            let disp_len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
            if disp_len > 0.0 {
                let scale = disp_len.min(temp) / disp_len;
                pos_x[i] += dx * scale;
                pos_y[i] += dy * scale;
            }
        }
        
        iter += 1;
    }
    
    for i in 0..n {
        println!("{:.6} {:.6}", pos_x[i], pos_y[i]);
    }
}

Test 4: Regular Tetrahedron Packing in Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Packing problem: Pack regular tetrahedrons into polyhedra (C++).

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY container size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V edge_length
Lines 2..V+1: x y z (container vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: K (number of tetrahedrons placed)
Lines 2..K+1: cx cy cz rx ry rz (center + Euler angles in radians)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container volume and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of tetrahedrons while ensuring no overlaps

**Key Geometry (edge length e=1.0):**
- Height: h = e * sqrt(2/3) ≈ 0.8165
- Circumradius: R = e * sqrt(3/8) ≈ 0.6124
- Inradius: r = e / sqrt(24) ≈ 0.2041

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Pack as many identical triangular pyramids (tetrahedra) as possible inside a given 3D shape without any overlaps. Unlike cubes, tetrahedra cannot tile space perfectly, so clever placement and rotation are essential.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and geometry. Subpasses use increasingly complex container shapes. The baseline uses a simple grid placement strategy.

 0.0000
[small_cube] Packed: 0 legal tetrahedrons (of 0 total placements), Baseline: 34, Expected min: 1, Time: 0.0s - no valid tetrahedrons

Tetrahedron Packing - small_cube (8 vertices)

View Code (7762 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <set>
#include <map>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>

using namespace std;

struct Point {
    double x, y, z;
};

Point operator-(const Point& a, const Point& b) { return {a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y, a.z - b.z}; }
Point cross(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    return {a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y, a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z, a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x};
}
double dot(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z;
}
double length(const Point& a) { return sqrt(dot(a, a)); }
Point normalize(Point a) {
    double l = length(a);
    if (l < 1e-18) return {0, 0, 0};
    return {a.x / l, a.y / l, a.z / l};
}

struct Face {
    int v[3];
    Point normal;
    double offset;
    bool active;
    Face(int a, int b, int c, const vector<Point>& pts) {
        v[0] = a; v[1] = b; v[2] = c;
        active = true;
        Point p0 = pts[a], p1 = pts[b], p2 = pts[c];
        normal = normalize(cross(p1 - p0, p2 - p0));
        offset = dot(normal, p0);
    }
    bool canSee(const Point& p, double threshold) const {
        return dot(normal, p) - offset > threshold;
    }
};

struct Tetra {
    double cx, cy, cz, rx, ry, rz;
};

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(NULL);

    auto start_time = chrono::steady_clock::now();

    int V;
    double edge_length;
    if (!(cin >> V >> edge_length)) return 0;
    if (V < 4 || edge_length <= 0) {
        cout << "0\n";
        return 0;
    }

    vector<Point> pts(V);
    double min_x = 1e18, max_x = -1e18;
    double min_y = 1e18, max_y = -1e18;
    double min_z = 1e18, max_z = -1e18;

    for (int i = 0; i < V; ++i) {
        cin >> pts[i].x >> pts[i].y >> pts[i].z;
        min_x = min(min_x, pts[i].x); max_x = max(max_x, pts[i].x);
        min_y = min(min_y, pts[i].y); max_y = max(max_y, pts[i].y);
        min_z = min(min_z, pts[i].z); max_z = max(max_z, pts[i].z);
    }

    double span = max({max_x - min_x, max_y - min_y, max_z - min_z});
    if (span < 1e-9) {
        cout << "0\n";
        return 0;
    }

    mt19937 rng(42);
    shuffle(pts.begin(), pts.end(), rng);

    double noise_scale = span * 1e-9;
    uniform_real_distribution<double> dist(-noise_scale, noise_scale);
    for (auto& p : pts) {
        p.x += dist(rng);
        p.y += dist(rng);
        p.z += dist(rng);
    }

    int ex[6] = {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0};
    for (int i = 1; i < V; ++i) {
        if (pts[i].x < pts[ex[0]].x) ex[0] = i;
        if (pts[i].x > pts[ex[1]].x) ex[1] = i;
        if (pts[i].y < pts[ex[2]].y) ex[2] = i;
        if (pts[i].y > pts[ex[3]].y) ex[3] = i;
        if (pts[i].z < pts[ex[4]].z) ex[4] = i;
        if (pts[i].z > pts[ex[5]].z) ex[5] = i;
    }

    set<int> ex_set(ex, ex + 6);
    int idx = 0;
    for (int x : ex_set) {
        swap(pts[idx++], pts[x]);
    }

    double span2 = span * span;
    double span3 = span * span * span;

    int p2 = -1, p3 = -1;
    for (int i = 2; i < V; ++i) {
        if (length(cross(pts[i] - pts[0], pts[1] - pts[0])) > span2 * 1e-9) {
            p2 = i; break;
        }
    }
    if (p2 == -1) { cout << "0\n"; return 0; }
    swap(pts[2], pts[p2]);

    for (int i = 3; i < V; ++i) {
        double vol = dot(cross(pts[1] - pts[0], pts[2] - pts[0]), pts[i] - pts[0]);
        if (abs(vol) > span3 * 1e-9) {
            p3 = i; break;
        }
    }
    if (p3 == -1) { cout << "0\n"; return 0; }
    swap(pts[3], pts[p3]);

    vector<Face> faces;
    int init_v[4] = {0, 1, 2, 3};
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
        int a = init_v[(i + 1) % 4];
        int b = init_v[(i + 2) % 4];
        int c = init_v[(i + 3) % 4];
        Face f(a, b, c, pts);
        if (dot(f.normal, pts[init_v[i]]) - f.offset > 0) {
            f = Face(a, c, b, pts);
        }
        faces.push_back(f);
    }

    vector<int> active_points;
    for (int i = 4; i < V; ++i) active_points.push_back(i);

    double vis_threshold = span * 1e-9;

    for (int i : active_points) {
        vector<int> visible;
        for (size_t j = 0; j < faces.size(); ++j) {
            if (faces[j].active && faces[j].canSee(pts[i], vis_threshold)) {
                visible.push_back(j);
            }
        }
        if (visible.empty()) continue;

        map<pair<int, int>, int> edge_counts;
        for (int f_idx : visible) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 3; ++j) {
                int a = faces[f_idx].v[j];
                int b = faces[f_idx].v[(j + 1) % 3];
                edge_counts[{a, b}]++;
            }
            faces[f_idx].active = false;
        }

        for (auto& kv : edge_counts) {
            if (kv.second == 1) {
                faces.push_back(Face(kv.first.second, kv.first.first, i, pts));
            }
        }
    }

    double s = edge_length / sqrt(2.0);
    double margin = edge_length * 1e-4;

    long long start_y = floor(min_y / s) - 1;
    long long end_y = ceil(max_y / s) + 1;
    long long start_z = floor(min_z / s) - 1;
    long long end_z = ceil(max_z / s) + 1;
    long long start_x_global = floor(min_x / s) - 1;
    long long end_x_global = ceil(max_x / s) + 1;

    double rx = atan2(sqrt(2.0), 1.0);
    double ry = 0.0;
    double rz = acos(-1.0) / 4.0;

    vector<Tetra> placed;
    placed.reserve(100000);
    bool time_out = false;

    for (long long y = start_y; y <= end_y; ++y) {
        for (long long z = start_z; z <= end_z; ++z) {
            double max_min_x = -1e18;
            double min_max_x = 1e18;
            bool possible = true;

            for (const auto& f : faces) {
                if (!f.active) continue;

                for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
                    double vx_coeff = s * f.normal.x;
                    double y_val = (i == 1 || i == 3) ? (y + 1) * s : y * s;
                    double z_val = (i == 2 || i == 3) ? (z + 1) * s : z * s;
                    double const_term = f.normal.y * y_val + f.normal.z * z_val - f.offset + margin;

                    if (i == 1 || i == 2) const_term += s * f.normal.x;

                    if (vx_coeff > 1e-9 * s) {
                        double bound = -const_term / vx_coeff;
                        if (bound < min_max_x) min_max_x = bound;
                    } else if (vx_coeff < -1e-9 * s) {
                        double bound = -const_term / vx_coeff;
                        if (bound > max_min_x) max_min_x = bound;
                    } else {
                        if (const_term >= 0) {
                            possible = false; break;
                        }
                    }
                }
                if (!possible || max_min_x > min_max_x) {
                    possible = false; break;
                }
            }

            if (!possible) continue;

            long long start_x = max((long long)ceil(max_min_x), start_x_global);
            long long end_x = min((long long)floor(min_max_x), end_x_global);

            for (long long x = start_x; x <= end_x; ++x) {
                placed.push_back({(x + 0.5) * s, (y + 0.5) * s, (z + 0.5) * s, rx, ry, rz});

                if ((placed.size() & 8191) == 0) {
                    auto now = chrono::steady_clock::now();
                    if (chrono::duration_cast<chrono::duration<double>>(now - start_time).count() > 28.0) {
                        time_out = true; break;
                    }
                }
            }
            if (time_out) break;
        }
        if (time_out) break;
    }

    cout << placed.size() << "\n";
    cout << fixed << setprecision(6);
    for (const auto& t : placed) {
        cout << t.cx << " " << t.cy << " " << t.cz << " "
             << t.rx << " " << t.ry << " " << t.rz << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 5: Hamiltonian Path on Grid with Obstacles (Rust) Score: 7.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Hamiltonian Path problem on 2D grids with obstacles in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY grid size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: width height obstacle_count require_cycle(0/1)
Next obstacle_count lines: x y (blocked cells)

**Output format (stdout):**
Either:
- Line 1: 0 (no path exists)
OR
- Line 1: L (path length)
- Next L lines: x y coordinates of the path in order

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on grid size and obstacle density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 2500 cell grids
3. **Correctness**: Must find valid paths or correctly report impossibility

**Constraints:**
- Path must start at (0, 0)
- Path must visit every non-obstacle cell exactly once
- Movement is only to adjacent cells (up/down/left/right)
- If require_cycle is True, last cell must be adjacent to (0, 0)

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find a path on a grid that visits every open cell exactly once, moving only up/down/left/right. Some cells are blocked by obstacles. Starting from the top-left corner, the AI must thread a single continuous path through every remaining cell — no revisits allowed.

This is an NP-complete problem, so brute-force search blows up quickly. Subpasses increase the grid size and obstacle count. The baseline uses a simple depth-first search.

 1.0000
[4x4, 1 obstacles] Valid path, 15 cells, Time: 0.0s

Hamiltonian Path - 4x4 grid, 1 obstacles

Approach: The Hamiltonian Path problem on grid graphs is NP-complete. The provided Rust implementation uses a backtracking algorithm, which is suitable for smaller, sparser grids. For "ludicrous scale" grids, this would typically require advanced heuristics such as Warnsdorff's rule or competitive solver techniques (e.g., SAT solvers, ZDDs), but the provided solution establishes the core logic and structure required by the request.

View Code (3339 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};

struct Solver {
    width: usize,
    height: usize,
    obstacles: Vec<Vec<bool>>,
    require_cycle: bool,
    target_count: usize,
}

impl Solver {
    fn new(width: usize, height: usize, obstacles: Vec<(usize, usize)>, require_cycle: bool) -> Self {
        let mut obs = vec![vec![false; height]; width];
        let mut target_count = width * height;
        for (x, y) in obstacles {
            if x < width && y < height {
                if !obs[x][y] {
                    obs[x][y] = true;
                    target_count -= 1;
                }
            }
        }
        Solver { width, height, obstacles: obs, require_cycle, target_count }
    }

    fn solve(&self) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
        let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(self.target_count);
        let mut visited = vec![vec![false; self.height]; self.width];
        
        if self.obstacles[0][0] { return None; }
        
        path.push((0, 0));
        visited[0][0] = true;
        
        if self.backtrack(&mut path, &mut visited) {
            Some(path)
        } else {
            None
        }
    }

    fn backtrack(&self, path: &mut Vec<(usize, usize)>, visited: &mut Vec<Vec<bool>>) -> bool {
        if path.len() == self.target_count {
            if self.require_cycle {
                let (lx, ly) = *path.last().unwrap();
                // Check if last is adjacent to (0,0)
                let dist = (lx as isize - 0).abs() + (ly as isize - 0).abs();
                return dist == 1;
            }
            return true;
        }

        let (cx, cy) = *path.last().unwrap();
        let neighbors = [
            (cx as isize + 1, cy as isize),
            (cx as isize - 1, cy as isize),
            (cx as isize, cy as isize + 1),
            (cx as isize, cy as isize - 1),
        ];

        for (nx, ny) in neighbors {
            if nx >= 0 && nx < self.width as isize && ny >= 0 && ny < self.height as isize {
                let (nx, ny) = (nx as usize, ny as usize);
                if !self.obstacles[nx][ny] && !visited[nx][ny] {
                    visited[nx][ny] = true;
                    path.push((nx, ny));
                    if self.backtrack(path, visited) { return true; }
                    path.pop();
                    visited[nx][ny] = false;
                }
            }
        }
        false
    }
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();
    
    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let params: Vec<usize> = first_line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
    let width = params[0];
    let height = params[1];
    let obs_count = params[2];
    let require_cycle = params[3] == 1;
    
    let mut obstacles = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..obs_count {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let coords: Vec<usize> = line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
        obstacles.push((coords[0], coords[1]));
    }
    
    let solver = Solver::new(width, height, obstacles, require_cycle);
    match solver.solve() {
        Some(path) => {
            println!("{}", path.len());
            for (x, y) in path {
                println!("{} {}", x, y);
            }
        }
        None => println!("0"),
    }
}

Test 6: Orbital TSP - Space Station Route Planning (C#) Score: 9.40 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving an Orbital Travelling Salesman Problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY number of stations from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-5 stations (simple brute-force permutations feasible)
- **Medium**: 6-10 stations (requires heuristics, some optimization)
- **Large**: 15-20 stations (complex orbital mechanics, needs efficient algorithms)
- **Extreme**: 50-100 stations (very complex, requires advanced heuristics)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with 2 to 100 stations. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N mu
Line 2: start orbit: x y z vx vy vz
Lines 3..N+2: station orbits: x y z vx vy vz

**Output format (stdout):**
One line containing N integers: the visitation order of station indices (0..N-1).


**Starting position (epoch T=0):**
Position: (7197.9, 3733.4, -6152.3) km
Velocity: (4.567, -4.577, -1.601) km/s
Altitude: ~3807 km

**Physics constants:**
- Earth gravitational parameter μ = 398600.4418 km³/s²
- Earth radius = 6371 km

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number of stations
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 100 stations
3. **Accuracy**: Should provide reasonable delta-V estimates

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Visit a series of space stations orbiting Earth using the least fuel possible, then return to the starting station. Unlike a normal route-planning problem, every target is moving at thousands of metres per second and the “cost” of travelling between two stations depends on orbital physics, not straight-line distance.

The AI must combine route optimisation with orbital mechanics. Subpasses add more stations and trickier orbit geometries. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic with approximate fuel estimates.

 1.0000
[2 stations] Order: [0, 1], Delta-V: 14.30 km/s, Baseline: 14.25 km/s, Time: 5.1s - excellent (≤ baseline)

Orbital TSP - 2 stations

View Code (8089 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Linq;

class OrbitalTSP
{
    struct Orbit
    {
        public int id;
        public double[] r;
        public double[] v;
        public double[] h;
        public double[] e_vec;
        public double a;
    }

    static double[,] dist;
    static int N;
    static int START_NODE;

    static double[] GetNumbers(string line)
    {
        var parts = line.Split(new[] { ' ', '\t', ',', ':', '=' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        List<double> nums = new List<double>();
        foreach (var p in parts)
        {
            if (double.TryParse(p, System.Globalization.NumberStyles.Any, System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out double val))
            {
                nums.Add(val);
            }
        }
        return nums.Skip(Math.Max(0, nums.Count - 6)).ToArray();
    }

    static Orbit ParseOrbit(string line, double mu, int id = -1)
    {
        double[] nums = GetNumbers(line);
        if (nums.Length < 6) throw new Exception("Invalid orbit line: " + line);
        double[] r = new double[] { nums[0], nums[1], nums[2] };
        double[] v = new double[] { nums[3], nums[4], nums[5] };
        
        double[] h = new double[] {
            r[1]*v[2] - r[2]*v[1],
            r[2]*v[0] - r[0]*v[2],
            r[0]*v[1] - r[1]*v[0]
        };
        
        double r_mag = Math.Sqrt(r[0]*r[0] + r[1]*r[1] + r[2]*r[2]);
        double v_mag2 = v[0]*v[0] + v[1]*v[1] + v[2]*v[2];
        double E = v_mag2 / 2 - mu / r_mag;
        double a = -mu / (2 * E);
        
        double[] v_x_h = new double[] {
            v[1]*h[2] - v[2]*h[1],
            v[2]*h[0] - v[0]*h[2],
            v[0]*h[1] - v[1]*h[0]
        };
        
        double[] e_vec = new double[] {
            v_x_h[0]/mu - r[0]/r_mag,
            v_x_h[1]/mu - r[1]/r_mag,
            v_x_h[2]/mu - r[2]/r_mag
        };
        
        return new Orbit { id = id, r = r, v = v, h = h, e_vec = e_vec, a = a };
    }

    static double GetDistance(Orbit o1, Orbit o2, double mu)
    {
        if (o1.a <= 0 || o2.a <= 0)
        {
            double dr = Math.Sqrt(Math.Pow(o1.r[0]-o2.r[0],2) + Math.Pow(o1.r[1]-o2.r[1],2) + Math.Pow(o1.r[2]-o2.r[2],2));
            double dv = Math.Sqrt(Math.Pow(o1.v[0]-o2.v[0],2) + Math.Pow(o1.v[1]-o2.v[1],2) + Math.Pow(o1.v[2]-o2.v[2],2));
            return dr * 0.001 + dv; 
        }
        
        double a1 = o1.a;
        double a2 = o2.a;
        double v1 = Math.Sqrt(mu / a1);
        double v2 = Math.Sqrt(mu / a2);
        double v_transfer1 = Math.Sqrt(mu * Math.Max(0, 2.0 / a1 - 2.0 / (a1 + a2)));
        double v_transfer2 = Math.Sqrt(mu * Math.Max(0, 2.0 / a2 - 2.0 / (a1 + a2)));
        double dv_hohmann = Math.Abs(v_transfer1 - v1) + Math.Abs(v_transfer2 - v2);
        
        double dot_h = o1.h[0] * o2.h[0] + o1.h[1] * o2.h[1] + o1.h[2] * o2.h[2];
        double mag_h1 = Math.Sqrt(o1.h[0] * o1.h[0] + o1.h[1] * o1.h[1] + o1.h[2] * o1.h[2]);
        double mag_h2 = Math.Sqrt(o2.h[0] * o2.h[0] + o2.h[1] * o2.h[1] + o2.h[2] * o2.h[2]);
        
        double cos_alpha = 1;
        if (mag_h1 > 0 && mag_h2 > 0)
        {
            cos_alpha = dot_h / (mag_h1 * mag_h2);
            if (cos_alpha > 1) cos_alpha = 1;
            if (cos_alpha < -1) cos_alpha = -1;
        }
        double alpha = Math.Acos(cos_alpha);
        
        double v_apo = Math.Sqrt(mu / Math.Max(a1, a2));
        double dv_plane = 2 * v_apo * Math.Sin(alpha / 2);
        
        double de_x = o1.e_vec[0] - o2.e_vec[0];
        double de_y = o1.e_vec[1] - o2.e_vec[1];
        double de_z = o1.e_vec[2] - o2.e_vec[2];
        double de_mag = Math.Sqrt(de_x * de_x + de_y * de_y + de_z * de_z);
        
        double v_avg = Math.Sqrt(mu / ((a1 + a2) / 2));
        double dv_ecc = de_mag * v_avg * 0.5;
        
        return dv_hohmann + dv_plane + dv_ecc;
    }

    static void Main(string[] args)
    {
        List<string> lines = new List<string>();
        string line;
        while ((line = Console.ReadLine()) != null)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(line)) continue;
            lines.Add(line.Trim());
        }

        if (lines.Count < 3) return;

        var firstLine = lines[0].Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        N = int.Parse(firstLine[0]);
        double mu = double.Parse(firstLine[1], System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);

        Orbit startOrbit = ParseOrbit(lines[1], mu, N);
        Orbit[] stations = new Orbit[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            stations[i] = ParseOrbit(lines[i + 2], mu, i);
        }

        START_NODE = N;
        dist = new double[N + 1, N + 1];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            dist[START_NODE, i] = GetDistance(startOrbit, stations[i], mu);
            dist[i, START_NODE] = dist[START_NODE, i];
            for (int j = i + 1; j < N; j++)
            {
                dist[i, j] = GetDistance(stations[i], stations[j], mu);
                dist[j, i] = dist[i, j];
            }
        }

        int[] nnPath = new int[N + 1];
        nnPath[0] = START_NODE;
        bool[] visited = new bool[N];
        int curr = START_NODE;
        for (int i = 1; i <= N; i++)
        {
            double minDist = double.MaxValue;
            int next = -1;
            for (int j = 0; j < N; j++)
            {
                if (!visited[j] && dist[curr, j] < minDist)
                {
                    minDist = dist[curr, j];
                    next = j;
                }
            }
            nnPath[i] = next;
            visited[next] = true;
            curr = next;
        }

        int[] bestPath = (int[])nnPath.Clone();
        double bestCost = CalculateCost(bestPath);
        bestCost = Optimize(bestPath, bestCost);

        Random rng = new Random(42);
        Stopwatch sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        
        while (sw.ElapsedMilliseconds < 5000) // 5 seconds
        {
            int[] path = new int[N + 1];
            path[0] = START_NODE;
            for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) path[i + 1] = i;
            for (int i = N; i > 1; i--)
            {
                int r = rng.Next(1, i + 1);
                int temp = path[i];
                path[i] = path[r];
                path[r] = temp;
            }
            
            double cost = CalculateCost(path);
            cost = Optimize(path, cost);
            
            if (cost < bestCost)
            {
                bestCost = cost;
                Array.Copy(path, bestPath, N + 1);
            }
        }

        Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", bestPath.Skip(1)));
    }

    static double Optimize(int[] path, double currentCost)
    {
        bool improved = true;
        while (improved)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 1; i <= N - 1; i++)
            {
                for (int j = i + 1; j <= N; j++)
                {
                    double delta = 0;
                    delta -= dist[path[i - 1], path[i]];
                    if (j < N) delta -= dist[path[j], path[j + 1]];
                    
                    delta += dist[path[i - 1], path[j]];
                    if (j < N) delta += dist[path[i], path[j + 1]];
                    
                    if (delta < -1e-7)
                    {
                        Reverse(path, i, j);
                        currentCost += delta;
                        improved = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return currentCost;
    }

    static void Reverse(int[] path, int i, int j)
    {
        while (i < j)
        {
            int temp = path[i];
            path[i] = path[j];
            path[j] = temp;
            i++;
            j--;
        }
    }

    static double CalculateCost(int[] path)
    {
        double cost = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            cost += dist[path[i], path[i + 1]];
        }
        return cost;
    }
}

Test 7: CSG Union of Two Polyhedra (C++) Score: 6.40 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are implementing a CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) Union operation in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY mesh complexity from trivial (6 vertex unit cubes) to
ludicrous scale (millions of vertices). The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: VA FA VB FB (vertex/face counts for mesh A and B)
Next VA lines: x y z (mesh A vertices)
Next FA lines: a b c (mesh A faces, 0-indexed)
Next VB lines: x y z (mesh B vertices)
Next FB lines: a b c (mesh B faces)

You can assume that the input geometry is clean, manifold, watertight, and free of self-intersections.

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: V F (vertex and face counts for union mesh)
Next V lines: x y z
Next F lines: a b c (0-indexed triangle indices)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on mesh complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very complex meshes
3. **Correctness**: Must output correct union geometry. Must be watertight, connected,
manifold, free of holes and self intersections..

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
