Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given two overlapping 3D shapes (triangle meshes), merge them into a single combined shape — like gluing two lumps of clay together and keeping only the outer surface. The output mesh must be watertight with no self-intersections.

The AI must correctly handle the tricky geometry where the two shapes overlap: splitting triangles, discarding interior faces, and stitching everything back together. Subpasses use increasingly complex shape pairs.

 1.0000
[overlapping_boxes] Result vol: 15.00, Expected (MC): ~15.04, Inputs: 8.00 + 8.00, Time: 0.0s - excellent (< 1% error). Topology: clean

CSG Union - overlapping_boxes

View Code (23876 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
#include <algorithm>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <random>

using namespace std;

struct vec3 {
    double x, y, z;
    vec3 operator-(const vec3& o) const { return {x - o.x, y - o.y, z - o.z}; }
    vec3 operator+(const vec3& o) const { return {x + o.x, y + o.y, z + o.z}; }
    vec3 operator*(double s) const { return {x * s, y * s, z * s}; }
    vec3& operator-=(const vec3& o) { x -= o.x; y -= o.y; z -= o.z; return *this; }
    vec3& operator+=(const vec3& o) { x += o.x; y += o.y; z += o.z; return *this; }
};

inline double dot(const vec3& a, const vec3& b) {
    return a.x*b.x + a.y*b.y + a.z*b.z;
}

inline vec3 cross(const vec3& a, const vec3& b) {
    return {a.y*b.z - a.z*b.y, a.z*b.x - a.x*b.z, a.x*b.y - a.y*b.x};
}

inline double length2(const vec3& a) {
    return dot(a, a);
}

inline double length(const vec3& a) {
    return sqrt(length2(a));
}

inline vec3 normalize(const vec3& a) {
    double l = length(a);
    return l > 1e-16 ? a * (1.0 / l) : vec3{1,0,0};
}

struct Triangle {
    int v[3];
};

struct AABB {
    vec3 bmin = {1e30, 1e30, 1e30};
    vec3 bmax = {-1e30, -1e30, -1e30};
    void expand(const vec3& p) {
        bmin.x = min(bmin.x, p.x); bmin.y = min(bmin.y, p.y); bmin.z = min(bmin.z, p.z);
        bmax.x = max(bmax.x, p.x); bmax.y = max(bmax.y, p.y); bmax.z = max(bmax.z, p.z);
    }
    bool intersects(const AABB& o) const {
        return (bmax.x >= o.bmin.x && bmin.x <= o.bmax.x &&
                bmax.y >= o.bmin.y && bmin.y <= o.bmax.y &&
                bmax.z >= o.bmin.z && bmin.z <= o.bmax.z);
    }
};

struct BVHNode {
    AABB box;
    int left, right;
    int tri_start, tri_count;
};

struct Mesh {
    vector<vec3> vertices;
    vector<Triangle> faces;
    vector<vec3> normals;
    
    vector<BVHNode> nodes;
    vector<int> bvh_tri_indices;
    int bvh_root = -1;
};

inline void compute_normals(Mesh& mesh) {
    mesh.normals.resize(mesh.faces.size());
    for (size_t i = 0; i < mesh.faces.size(); ++i) {
        vec3 v0 = mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[i].v[0]];
        vec3 v1 = mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[i].v[1]];
        vec3 v2 = mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[i].v[2]];
        mesh.normals[i] = normalize(cross(v1 - v0, v2 - v0));
    }
}

int build_bvh(vector<int>& tri_indices, int start, int count, Mesh& mesh) {
    AABB box;
    for (int i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        int idx = tri_indices[start + i];
        box.expand(mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[idx].v[0]]);
        box.expand(mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[idx].v[1]]);
        box.expand(mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[idx].v[2]]);
    }
    
    if (count <= 4) {
        mesh.nodes.push_back({box, -1, -1, start, count});
        return mesh.nodes.size() - 1;
    }
    
    vec3 extent = box.bmax - box.bmin;
    int axis = 0;
    if (extent.y > extent.x) axis = 1;
    if (extent.z > extent.y && extent.z > extent.x) axis = 2;
    
    std::sort(tri_indices.begin() + start, tri_indices.begin() + start + count, [&](int a, int b) {
        vec3 ca = (mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[a].v[0]] + mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[a].v[1]] + mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[a].v[2]]) * (1.0/3.0);
        vec3 cb = (mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[b].v[0]] + mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[b].v[1]] + mesh.vertices[mesh.faces[b].v[2]]) * (1.0/3.0);
        if (axis == 0) return ca.x < cb.x;
        if (axis == 1) return ca.y < cb.y;
        return ca.z < cb.z;
    });
    
    int mid = count / 2;
    int node_idx = mesh.nodes.size();
    mesh.nodes.push_back({box, -1, -1, -1, -1}); 
    
    int left = build_bvh(tri_indices, start, mid, mesh);
    int right = build_bvh(tri_indices, start + mid, count - mid, mesh);
    
    mesh.nodes[node_idx].left = left;
    mesh.nodes[node_idx].right = right;
    
    return node_idx;
}

void query_bvh(int bvh_root, const AABB& box, const Mesh& mesh, vector<int>& result) {
    if (bvh_root == -1) return;
    vector<int> stack;
    stack.push_back(bvh_root);
    while (!stack.empty()) {
        int node_idx = stack.back();
        stack.pop_back();
        if (!mesh.nodes[node_idx].box.intersects(box)) continue;
        
        if (mesh.nodes[node_idx].left == -1) {
            for (int i = 0; i < mesh.nodes[node_idx].tri_count; ++i) {
                result.push_back(mesh.bvh_tri_indices[mesh.nodes[node_idx].tri_start + i]);
            }
        } else {
            stack.push_back(mesh.nodes[node_idx].left);
            stack.push_back(mesh.nodes[node_idx].right);
        }
    }
}

inline bool tri_tri_intersect_fast(const vec3& V0, const vec3& V1, const vec3& V2,
                                   const vec3& U0, const vec3& U1, const vec3& U2, double eps) {
    vec3 N1 = cross(V1 - V0, V2 - V0);
    double len1 = length(N1);
    if (len1 < 1e-16) return false;
    N1 = N1 * (1.0 / len1);
    double d1 = dot(N1, V0);
    
    double du0 = dot(N1, U0) - d1;
    double du1 = dot(N1, U1) - d1;
    double du2 = dot(N1, U2) - d1;
    if ((du0 > eps && du1 > eps && du2 > eps) ||
        (du0 < -eps && du1 < -eps && du2 < -eps)) return false;
        
    vec3 N2 = cross(U1 - U0, U2 - U0);
    double len2 = length(N2);
    if (len2 < 1e-16) return false;
    N2 = N2 * (1.0 / len2);
    double d2 = dot(N2, U0);
    
    double dv0 = dot(N2, V0) - d2;
    double dv1 = dot(N2, V1) - d2;
    double dv2 = dot(N2, V2) - d2;
    if ((dv0 > eps && dv1 > eps && dv2 > eps) ||
        (dv0 < -eps && dv1 < -eps && dv2 < -eps)) return false;
        
    return true;
}

void split_polygon(const vector<vec3>& P, const vec3& N, double d, double eps, vector<vec3>& P_front, vector<vec3>& P_back) {
    int n = P.size();
    if (n == 0) return;
    vector<double> dists(n);
    int pos = 0, neg = 0;
    for (int i=0; i<n; ++i) {
        dists[i] = dot(N, P[i]) - d;
        if (dists[i] > eps) pos++;
        else if (dists[i] < -eps) neg++;
        else dists[i] = 0; 
    }
    if (pos == 0 && neg == 0) {
        P_front = P; return;
    }
    if (pos == 0) { P_back = P; return; }
    if (neg == 0) { P_front = P; return; }
    
    for (int i=0; i<n; ++i) {
        int j = (i + 1) % n;
        double d1 = dists[i], d2 = dists[j];
        vec3 p1 = P[i], p2 = P[j];
        
        if (d1 >= 0) P_front.push_back(p1);
        if (d1 <= 0) P_back.push_back(p1);
        
        if ((d1 > 0 && d2 < 0) || (d1 < 0 && d2 > 0)) {
            double t = d1 / (d1 - d2);
            vec3 p_intersect = p1 + (p2 - p1) * t;
            P_front.push_back(p_intersect);
            P_back.push_back(p_intersect);
        }
    }
}

void clean_polygon(vector<vec3>& P, double eps) {
    if (P.empty()) return;
    vector<vec3> res;
    res.push_back(P[0]);
    for (size_t i = 1; i < P.size(); ++i) {
        if (length2(P[i] - res.back()) > eps*eps) {
            res.push_back(P[i]);
        }
    }
    while (res.size() > 1 && length2(res.back() - res[0]) <= eps*eps) {
        res.pop_back();
    }
    if (res.size() < 3) P.clear();
    else P = res;
}

inline double point_triangle_sq_dist(const vec3& p, const vec3& a, const vec3& b, const vec3& c) {
    vec3 ab = b - a;
    vec3 ac = c - a;
    vec3 ap = p - a;
    double d1 = dot(ab, ap);
    double d2 = dot(ac, ap);
    if (d1 <= 0.0 && d2 <= 0.0) return length2(ap);
    vec3 bp = p - b;
    double d3 = dot(ab, bp);
    double d4 = dot(ac, bp);
    if (d3 >= 0.0 && d4 <= d3) return length2(bp);
    double vc = d1*d4 - d3*d2;
    if (vc <= 0.0 && d1 >= 0.0 && d3 <= 0.0) {
        double v = d1 / (d1 - d3);
        return length2(a + ab * v - p);
    }
    vec3 cp = p - c;
    double d5 = dot(ab, cp);
    double d6 = dot(ac, cp);
    if (d6 >= 0.0 && d5 <= d6) return length2(cp);
    double vb = d5*d2 - d1*d6;
    if (vb <= 0.0 && d2 >= 0.0 && d6 <= 0.0) {
        double w = d2 / (d2 - d6);
        return length2(a + ac * w - p);
    }
    double va = d3*d6 - d5*d4;
    if (va <= 0.0 && (d4 - d3) >= 0.0 && (d5 - d6) >= 0.0) {
        double w = (d4 - d3) / ((d4 - d3) + (d5 - d6));
        return length2(b + (c - b) * w - p);
    }
    double denom = 1.0 / (va + vb + vc);
    double v = vb * denom;
    double w = vc * denom;
    return length2(a + ab * v + ac * w - p);
}

inline bool ray_aabb_intersect(const vec3& orig, const vec3& inv_dir, const AABB& box) {
    double tx1 = (box.bmin.x - orig.x) * inv_dir.x;
    double tx2 = (box.bmax.x - orig.x) * inv_dir.x;
    double tmin = min(tx1, tx2);
    double tmax = max(tx1, tx2);
    
    double ty1 = (box.bmin.y - orig.y) * inv_dir.y;
    double ty2 = (box.bmax.y - orig.y) * inv_dir.y;
    tmin = max(tmin, min(ty1, ty2));
    tmax = min(tmax, max(ty1, ty2));
    
    double tz1 = (box.bmin.z - orig.z) * inv_dir.z;
    double tz2 = (box.bmax.z - orig.z) * inv_dir.z;
    tmin = max(tmin, min(tz1, tz2));
    tmax = min(tmax, max(tz1, tz2));
    
    return tmax >= max(0.0, tmin);
}

inline bool ray_triangle_intersect(const vec3& orig, const vec3& dir,
                                   const vec3& v0, const vec3& v1, const vec3& v2,
                                   double& t, double& u, double& v) {
    vec3 edge1 = v1 - v0;
    vec3 edge2 = v2 - v0;
    vec3 pvec = cross(dir, edge2);
    double det = dot(edge1, pvec);
    if (abs(det) < 1e-12) return false;
    double inv_det = 1.0 / det;
    vec3 tvec = orig - v0;
    u = dot(tvec, pvec) * inv_det;
    if (u < 0.0 || u > 1.0) return false;
    vec3 qvec = cross(tvec, edge1);
    v = dot(dir, qvec) * inv_det;
    if (v < 0.0 || u + v > 1.0) return false;
    t = dot(edge2, qvec) * inv_det;
    return true;
}

vec3 random_dir() {
    static mt19937 gen(1337);
    static uniform_real_distribution<double> dist(-1.0, 1.0);
    while (true) {
        vec3 v = {dist(gen), dist(gen), dist(gen)};
        double l2 = length2(v);
        if (l2 > 0.01 && l2 <= 1.0) {
            return v * (1.0 / sqrt(l2));
        }
    }
}

enum class RayHit { INSIDE, OUTSIDE, ON_BOUNDARY };

struct ClassifyResult {
    RayHit type;
    int closest_tri_idx;
};

ClassifyResult classify_point(const vec3& p, int bvh_root, const Mesh& mesh, double eps) {
    AABB p_box;
    p_box.bmin = p - vec3{eps, eps, eps};
    p_box.bmax = p + vec3{eps, eps, eps};
    vector<int> close_tris;
    query_bvh(bvh_root, p_box, mesh, close_tris);
    
    double min_dist = eps * eps;
    int closest_idx = -1;
    
    for (int t_idx : close_tris) {
        const auto& tri = mesh.faces[t_idx];
        vec3 v0 = mesh.vertices[tri.v[0]];
        vec3 v1 = mesh.vertices[tri.v[1]];
        vec3 v2 = mesh.vertices[tri.v[2]];
        double d2 = point_triangle_sq_dist(p, v0, v1, v2);
        if (d2 < min_dist) {
            min_dist = d2;
            closest_idx = t_idx;
        }
    }
    
    if (closest_idx != -1) {
        return {RayHit::ON_BOUNDARY, closest_idx};
    }
    
    for (int attempt = 0; attempt < 10; ++attempt) {
        vec3 dir = random_dir();
        vec3 inv_dir = {1.0/dir.x, 1.0/dir.y, 1.0/dir.z};
        int hit_count = 0;
        bool retry = false;
        
        vector<int> stack;
        stack.push_back(bvh_root);
        
        while (!stack.empty()) {
            int node_idx = stack.back();
            stack.pop_back();
            
            if (!ray_aabb_intersect(p, inv_dir, mesh.nodes[node_idx].box)) continue;
            
            if (mesh.nodes[node_idx].left == -1) {
                for (int i = 0; i < mesh.nodes[node_idx].tri_count; ++i) {
                    int t_idx = mesh.bvh_tri_indices[mesh.nodes[node_idx].tri_start + i];
                    const auto& tri = mesh.faces[t_idx];
                    vec3 v0 = mesh.vertices[tri.v[0]];
                    vec3 v1 = mesh.vertices[tri.v[1]];
                    vec3 v2 = mesh.vertices[tri.v[2]];
                    
                    double t, u, v;
                    if (ray_triangle_intersect(p, dir, v0, v1, v2, t, u, v)) {
                        if (t > eps) {
                            if (u < 1e-4 || v < 1e-4 || (1.0 - u - v) < 1e-4) {
                                retry = true; break;
                            }
                            hit_count++;
                        }
                    }
                }
            } else {
                stack.push_back(mesh.nodes[node_idx].left);
                stack.push_back(mesh.nodes[node_idx].right);
            }
            if (retry) break;
        }
        
        if (!retry) {
            return { (hit_count % 2 == 1) ? RayHit::INSIDE : RayHit::OUTSIDE, -1 };
        }
    }
    return {RayHit::OUTSIDE, -1};
}

struct Grid {
    double cell_size;
    unordered_map<int64_t, vector<int>> cells;
    
    Grid(double s) : cell_size(s) {}
    
    int64_t hash(int64_t x, int64_t y, int64_t z) const {
        return (x * 73856093) ^ (y * 19349663) ^ (z * 83492791);
    }
    
    void insert(const vec3& p, int idx) {
        int64_t x = std::floor(p.x / cell_size);
        int64_t y = std::floor(p.y / cell_size);
        int64_t z = std::floor(p.z / cell_size);
        cells[hash(x, y, z)].push_back(idx);
    }
    
    vector<int> query(const vec3& p, double radius) const {
        vector<int> res;
        int64_t x0 = std::floor((p.x - radius) / cell_size);
        int64_t x1 = std::floor((p.x + radius) / cell_size);
        int64_t y0 = std::floor((p.y - radius) / cell_size);
        int64_t y1 = std::floor((p.y + radius) / cell_size);
        int64_t z0 = std::floor((p.z - radius) / cell_size);
        int64_t z1 = std::floor((p.z + radius) / cell_size);
        
        for (int64_t x = x0; x <= x1; ++x) {
            for (int64_t y = y0; y <= y1; ++y) {
                for (int64_t z = z0; z <= z1; ++z) {
                    auto it = cells.find(hash(x, y, z));
                    if (it != cells.end()) {
                        res.insert(res.end(), it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return res;
    }
    
    vector<int> query(const AABB& box, const vector<vec3>& vertices) const {
        vector<int> res;
        int64_t x0 = std::floor(box.bmin.x / cell_size);
        int64_t x1 = std::floor(box.bmax.x / cell_size);
        int64_t y0 = std::floor(box.bmin.y / cell_size);
        int64_t y1 = std::floor(box.bmax.y / cell_size);
        int64_t z0 = std::floor(box.bmin.z / cell_size);
        int64_t z1 = std::floor(box.bmax.z / cell_size);
        
        int64_t num_cells = (x1 - x0 + 1) * (y1 - y0 + 1) * (z1 - z0 + 1);
        if (num_cells > 8000) {
            for (const auto& pair : cells) {
                for (int idx : pair.second) {
                    const vec3& p = vertices[idx];
                    if (p.x >= box.bmin.x && p.x <= box.bmax.x &&
                        p.y >= box.bmin.y && p.y <= box.bmax.y &&
                        p.z >= box.bmin.z && p.z <= box.bmax.z) {
                        res.push_back(idx);
                    }
                }
            }
            return res;
        }
        
        for (int64_t x = x0; x <= x1; ++x) {
            for (int64_t y = y0; y <= y1; ++y) {
                for (int64_t z = z0; z <= z1; ++z) {
                    auto it = cells.find(hash(x, y, z));
                    if (it != cells.end()) {
                        for (int idx : it->second) {
                            const vec3& p = vertices[idx];
                            if (p.x >= box.bmin.x && p.x <= box.bmax.x &&
                                p.y >= box.bmin.y && p.y <= box.bmax.y &&
                                p.z >= box.bmin.z && p.z <= box.bmax.z) {
                                res.push_back(idx);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return res;
    }
};

struct TriangleVec3 {
    vec3 v[3];
};

double dist_point_segment(const vec3& p, const vec3& a, const vec3& b) {
    vec3 ab = b - a;
    double l2 = length2(ab);
    if (l2 < 1e-16) return length(p - a);
    double t = dot(p - a, ab) / l2;
    if (t <= 0.0) return length(p - a);
    if (t >= 1.0) return length(p - b);
    return length(p - (a + ab * t));
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(NULL);
    
    int VA, FA, VB, FB;
    if (!(cin >> VA >> FA >> VB >> FB)) return 0;
    
    Mesh mesh_A;
    mesh_A.vertices.resize(VA);
    mesh_A.faces.resize(FA);
    for (int i=0; i<VA; ++i) cin >> mesh_A.vertices[i].x >> mesh_A.vertices[i].y >> mesh_A.vertices[i].z;
    for (int i=0; i<FA; ++i) cin >> mesh_A.faces[i].v[0] >> mesh_A.faces[i].v[1] >> mesh_A.faces[i].v[2];
    
    Mesh mesh_B;
    mesh_B.vertices.resize(VB);
    mesh_B.faces.resize(FB);
    for (int i=0; i<VB; ++i) cin >> mesh_B.vertices[i].x >> mesh_B.vertices[i].y >> mesh_B.vertices[i].z;
    for (int i=0; i<FB; ++i) cin >> mesh_B.faces[i].v[0] >> mesh_B.faces[i].v[1] >> mesh_B.faces[i].v[2];

    AABB global_box;
    for (const auto& v : mesh_A.vertices) global_box.expand(v);
    for (const auto& v : mesh_B.vertices) global_box.expand(v);
    
    vec3 extent = global_box.bmax - global_box.bmin;
    double max_extent = max({extent.x, extent.y, extent.z});
    double eps = max_extent * 1e-9;
    if (eps < 1e-12) eps = 1e-12;
    
    compute_normals(mesh_A);
    compute_normals(mesh_B);
    
    mesh_A.bvh_tri_indices.resize(FA);
    for (int i=0; i<FA; ++i) mesh_A.bvh_tri_indices[i] = i;
    mesh_A.bvh_root = build_bvh(mesh_A.bvh_tri_indices, 0, FA, mesh_A);
    
    mesh_B.bvh_tri_indices.resize(FB);
    for (int i=0; i<FB; ++i) mesh_B.bvh_tri_indices[i] = i;
    mesh_B.bvh_root = build_bvh(mesh_B.bvh_tri_indices, 0, FB, mesh_B);
    
    vector<TriangleVec3> soup;
    
    auto process_mesh = [&](const Mesh& mA, const Mesh& mB, bool is_A) {
        for (int i = 0; i < mA.faces.size(); ++i) {
            const auto& tri = mA.faces[i];
            vec3 v0 = mA.vertices[tri.v[0]];
            vec3 v1 = mA.vertices[tri.v[1]];
            vec3 v2 = mA.vertices[tri.v[2]];
            
            AABB box;
            box.expand(v0); box.expand(v1); box.expand(v2);
            box.bmin -= vec3{eps, eps, eps};
            box.bmax += vec3{eps, eps, eps};
            
            vector<int> close_tris;
            query_bvh(mB.bvh_root, box, mB, close_tris);
            
            vector<int> intersecting_tris;
            for (int t_idx : close_tris) {
                const auto& tri_B = mB.faces[t_idx];
                vec3 u0 = mB.vertices[tri_B.v[0]];
                vec3 u1 = mB.vertices[tri_B.v[1]];
                vec3 u2 = mB.vertices[tri_B.v[2]];
                if (tri_tri_intersect_fast(v0, v1, v2, u0, u1, u2, eps)) {
                    intersecting_tris.push_back(t_idx);
                }
            }
            
            vector<vector<vec3>> P_list = {{v0, v1, v2}};
            
            for (int t_idx : intersecting_tris) {
                vec3 N_B = mB.normals[t_idx];
                double d_B = dot(N_B, mB.vertices[mB.faces[t_idx].v[0]]);
                vector<vector<vec3>> new_P_list;
                for (auto& P : P_list) {
                    vector<vec3> P_front, P_back;
                    split_polygon(P, N_B, d_B, eps, P_front, P_back);
                    clean_polygon(P_front, eps);
                    clean_polygon(P_back, eps);
                    if (!P_front.empty()) new_P_list.push_back(P_front);
                    if (!P_back.empty()) new_P_list.push_back(P_back);
                }
                P_list = new_P_list;
            }
            
            for (auto& P : P_list) {
                for (size_t k = 1; k + 1 < P.size(); ++k) {
                    vec3 pv0 = P[0], pv1 = P[k], pv2 = P[k+1];
                    vec3 C = (pv0 + pv1 + pv2) * (1.0/3.0);
                    vec3 N_sub = cross(pv1 - pv0, pv2 - pv0);
                    if (length2(N_sub) < 1e-16) continue;
                    N_sub = normalize(N_sub);
                    
                    ClassifyResult res = classify_point(C, mB.bvh_root, mB, eps);
                    bool keep = false;
                    
                    if (res.type == RayHit::ON_BOUNDARY) {
                        vec3 N_closest = mB.normals[res.closest_tri_idx];
                        if (dot(N_sub, N_closest) > 0.0) {
                            keep = is_A;
                        }
                    } else if (res.type == RayHit::OUTSIDE) {
                        keep = true;
                    }
                    
                    if (keep) {
                        soup.push_back({{pv0, pv1, pv2}});
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    };
    
    process_mesh(mesh_A, mesh_B, true);
    process_mesh(mesh_B, mesh_A, false);
    
    vector<vec3> out_v;
    vector<Triangle> out_tris;
    Grid weld_grid(eps * 5.0);
    
    for (const auto& tri : soup) {
        Triangle t_idx;
        for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
            vec3 p = tri.v[i];
            vector<int> neighbors = weld_grid.query(p, eps);
            int match = -1;
            for (int n : neighbors) {
                if (length2(out_v[n] - p) < eps*eps) {
                    match = n; break;
                }
            }
            if (match != -1) {
                t_idx.v[i] = match;
            } else {
                t_idx.v[i] = out_v.size();
                weld_grid.insert(p, out_v.size());
                out_v.push_back(p);
            }
        }
        if (t_idx.v[0] != t_idx.v[1] && t_idx.v[1] != t_idx.v[2] && t_idx.v[2] != t_idx.v[0]) {
            out_tris.push_back(t_idx);
        }
    }
    
    double avg_len = 0;
    int count = 0;
    for (const auto& tri : out_tris) {
        vec3 v0 = out_v[tri.v[0]];
        vec3 v1 = out_v[tri.v[1]];
        vec3 v2 = out_v[tri.v[2]];
        avg_len += length(v1 - v0) + length(v2 - v1) + length(v0 - v2);
        count += 3;
    }
    if (count > 0) avg_len /= count;
    double t_cell_size = max(avg_len, eps * 10.0);
    
    Grid t_grid(t_cell_size);
    for (size_t i = 0; i < out_v.size(); ++i) {
        t_grid.insert(out_v[i], i);
    }
    
    vector<Triangle> final_tris;
    vector<Triangle> pending = out_tris;
    
    while (!pending.empty()) {
        Triangle tri = pending.back();
        pending.pop_back();
        
        vec3 v0 = out_v[tri.v[0]];
        vec3 v1 = out_v[tri.v[1]];
        vec3 v2 = out_v[tri.v[2]];
        AABB box;
        box.expand(v0); box.expand(v1); box.expand(v2);
        box.bmin -= vec3{eps, eps, eps};
        box.bmax += vec3{eps, eps, eps};
        
        vector<int> candidates = t_grid.query(box, out_v);
        bool split_tri = false;
        
        for (int w_idx : candidates) {
            if (w_idx == tri.v[0] || w_idx == tri.v[1] || w_idx == tri.v[2]) continue;
            vec3 w = out_v[w_idx];
            
            if (dist_point_segment(w, v0, v1) < eps) {
                pending.push_back({tri.v[0], w_idx, tri.v[2]});
                pending.push_back({w_idx, tri.v[1], tri.v[2]});
                split_tri = true; break;
            }
            if (dist_point_segment(w, v1, v2) < eps) {
                pending.push_back({tri.v[1], w_idx, tri.v[0]});
                pending.push_back({w_idx, tri.v[2], tri.v[0]});
                split_tri = true; break;
            }
            if (dist_point_segment(w, v2, v0) < eps) {
                pending.push_back({tri.v[2], w_idx, tri.v[1]});
                pending.push_back({w_idx, tri.v[0], tri.v[1]});
                split_tri = true; break;
            }
        }
        
        if (!split_tri) {
            final_tris.push_back(tri);
        }
    }
    
    cout << out_v.size() << " " << final_tris.size() << "\n";
    for (const auto& v : out_v) {
        cout << v.x << " " << v.y << " " << v.z << "\n";
    }
    for (const auto& t : final_tris) {
        cout << t.v[0] << " " << t.v[1] << " " << t.v[2] << "\n";
    }
    
    return 0;
}

Test 8: Maze Solver (Rust) Score: 14.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a maze solver in Rust that must handle ANY maze complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

Your program will be tested with mazes ranging from 5x5 to 50000x50000.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Maze format:**
- 'A' = Start position
- 'B' = Goal/End position
- '#' = Wall (cannot pass through)
- ' ' (space) = Open path (can walk through)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the maze row strings (each W characters)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of steps in path)
Next N lines: x y (0-indexed coordinates from top-left)
Path should include both start (A) and end (B) positions.
Movement is only up/down/left/right (no diagonals).
Output nothing (or 0) if no path exists.

**Constraints:**
- Path must be continuous (each step adjacent to previous)
- Path must not go through walls ('#')
- Path must start at 'A' and end at 'B'
- Path must not loop even if the maze allows it.
- If multiple solutions exist, return any one.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the shortest path through a maze from a start cell to an end cell. The maze is a grid of walls and open passages, and the AI must navigate from one corner to the other using the fewest steps possible.

Subpasses increase the maze size dramatically, testing whether the AI's approach scales to very large mazes. The baseline finds the true shortest path using breadth-first search.

 1.0000
[5x5] Path found, Time: 0.0s - path length 5

Maze Solver - 5x5

Algorithm: Here is a complete, highly optimized Rust solution designed specifically to handle ludicrously scaled mazes efficiently. ### Strategy and Optimizations: 1. **0-1 BFS with Manhattan Heuristic (A* equivalent):** Finding a path in a $50000 \times 50000$ empty space using standard BFS would require probing 2.5 billion cells. This solution uses a highly-tuned 0-1 BFS (with two stacks) driven by a dynamic Manhattan distance check. This creates an $O(1)$ constant-time bucketed A* search that will dar...

View Code (5955 chars)
use std::io::{BufRead, Write};

fn main() {
    let stdin = std::io::stdin();
    let mut stdin_lock = stdin.lock();
    
    // 1. Parse Dimensions
    let mut first_line = String::new();
    if stdin_lock.read_line(&mut first_line).unwrap_or(0) == 0 {
        return;
    }
    let parts: Vec<&str> = first_line.trim().split_whitespace().collect();
    if parts.len() < 2 { return; }
    let h: u32 = parts[0].parse().unwrap();
    let w: u32 = parts[1].parse().unwrap();
    
    // 2. Stream & Flatten the Maze File directly into a 1D Array
    let mut maze = Vec::with_capacity((h * w) as usize);
    let mut line_buf = Vec::with_capacity((w + 10) as usize);
    let mut idx = 0;
    let mut start_idx = u32::MAX;
    let mut end_idx = u32::MAX;
    
    for _ in 0..h {
        line_buf.clear();
        stdin_lock.read_until(b'\n', &mut line_buf).unwrap();
        
        let mut added = 0;
        for &b in &line_buf {
            if b == b'\n' || b == b'\r' {
                continue;
            }
            if added < w {
                maze.push(b);
                if b == b'A' {
                    start_idx = idx;
                } else if b == b'B' {
                    end_idx = idx;
                }
                idx += 1;
                added += 1;
            }
        }
        
        // Pad line with spaces if it's abruptly short
        while added < w {
            maze.push(b' ');
            idx += 1;
            added += 1;
        }
    }
    
    if start_idx == u32::MAX || end_idx == u32::MAX {
        println!("0");
        return;
    }
    
    let end_x = end_idx % w;
    let end_y = end_idx / w;

    // 0-1 BFS Priority Queues
    let cap = std::cmp::min(1_000_000, h.saturating_mul(w));
    let mut q0: Vec<(u32, u32, u8)> = Vec::with_capacity(cap as usize);
    let mut q2: Vec<(u32, u32, u8)> = Vec::with_capacity(cap as usize);
    
    let start_x = start_idx % w;
    let start_y = start_idx / w;
    
    q0.push((start_x, start_y, b'S'));
    
    let mut found = false;
    
    // 3. Search Loop
    while !q0.is_empty() || !q2.is_empty() {
        let (x, y, dir) = if let Some(val) = q0.pop() {
            val
        } else {
            std::mem::swap(&mut q0, &mut q2);
            q0.pop().unwrap()
        };
        
        let u = y * w + x;
        // Access unchecked safely because boundaries are implicitly restricted.
        let cell = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(u as usize) };
        if cell != b' ' && cell != b'A' && cell != b'B' {
            continue; // Node already visited through a more optimal path
        }
        
        // Store our backtracking direction into the maze board dynamically.
        unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked_mut(u as usize) = dir; }
        
        if u == end_idx {
            found = true;
            break;
        }
        
        // Up
        if y > 0 {
            let ny = y - 1;
            let nx = x;
            let v = ny * w + nx;
            let c = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(v as usize) };
            if c == b' ' || c == b'B' {
                let w_mod = if y > end_y { 0 } else { 2 };
                if w_mod == 0 { q0.push((nx, ny, b'U')); } else { q2.push((nx, ny, b'U')); }
            }
        }
        // Down
        if y + 1 < h {
            let ny = y + 1;
            let nx = x;
            let v = ny * w + nx;
            let c = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(v as usize) };
            if c == b' ' || c == b'B' {
                let w_mod = if y < end_y { 0 } else { 2 };
                if w_mod == 0 { q0.push((nx, ny, b'D')); } else { q2.push((nx, ny, b'D')); }
            }
        }
        // Left
        if x > 0 {
            let ny = y;
            let nx = x - 1;
            let v = ny * w + nx;
            let c = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(v as usize) };
            if c == b' ' || c == b'B' {
                let w_mod = if x > end_x { 0 } else { 2 };
                if w_mod == 0 { q0.push((nx, ny, b'L')); } else { q2.push((nx, ny, b'L')); }
            }
        }
        // Right
        if x + 1 < w {
            let ny = y;
            let nx = x + 1;
            let v = ny * w + nx;
            let c = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(v as usize) };
            if c == b' ' || c == b'B' {
                let w_mod = if x < end_x { 0 } else { 2 };
                if w_mod == 0 { q0.push((nx, ny, b'R')); } else { q2.push((nx, ny, b'R')); }
            }
        }
    }
    
    // 4. Trace the Solution Path Out
    let stdout = std::io::stdout();
    let mut handle = std::io::BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
    
    if found {
        let mut path_len = 0;
        let mut curr = end_idx;
        
        // Compute strict length so we can pre-allocate path exactly (avoiding re-alloc overheads).
        loop {
            path_len += 1;
            let dir = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(curr as usize) };
            if dir == b'S' { break; }
            curr = match dir {
                b'U' => curr + w,
                b'D' => curr - w,
                b'L' => curr + 1,
                b'R' => curr - 1,
                _ => unreachable!(),
            };
        }
        
        let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(path_len as usize);
        curr = end_idx;
        loop {
            path.push(curr);
            let dir = unsafe { *maze.get_unchecked(curr as usize) };
            if dir == b'S' { break; }
            curr = match dir {
                b'U' => curr + w,
                b'D' => curr - w,
                b'L' => curr + 1,
                b'R' => curr - 1,
                _ => unreachable!(),
            };
        }
        
        path.reverse();
        
        writeln!(handle, "{}", path.len()).unwrap();
        for idx in path {
            let x = idx % w;
            let y = idx / w;
            writeln!(handle, "{} {}", x, y).unwrap();
        }
    } else {
        writeln!(handle, "0").unwrap();
    }
}

Test 9: 1D Cutting Stock - Minimum Waste (C#) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 1D Cutting Stock Problem in C#.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N stock_length
Line 2: N cut lengths separated by spaces

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next num_stocks lines: space-separated cut indices assigned to that stock (0-indexed)

**Example:**
For cuts=[30, 40, 50] and stock_length=100:
Input:
3 100
30 40 50

Output:
2
0 1
2

**Constraints:**
- Each cut must come from a single stock piece (no gluing)
- Multiple cuts can come from the same stock piece if they fit
- No cut can exceed stock_length
- Must handle varying numbers of cuts efficiently. You can use
multiple threads, you can micro-optimise code, you can write clever algorithms,
whatever it takes to handle the problem and solve it within 5 minutes even if
there are millions of cuts.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.5.0-2.26208.13 (3dc781932f83b0792b0d1717a68db3f9604a2cb1)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given fixed-length stock material and a shopping list of smaller pieces to cut, use as few stock pieces as possible. Every requested piece must be cut from somewhere, but any leftover material is wasted — so packing the cuts tightly matters.

This is essentially a bin-packing problem and is NP-hard. Subpasses scale from a handful of cuts up to millions, forcing the AI to write code that is both correct and fast. The baseline uses a simple first-fit-decreasing heuristic.

 0.0000
[5 cuts, stock=100] Compilation failed:
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmp_nbo2v0i.cs(34,15): error CS0117: 'Array' does not contain a definition for 'Fill'

Cutting Stock - 5 cuts, stock=100

View Code (6111 chars)
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        using var stream = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        var scanner = new FastScanner(stream);
        
        int n = scanner.ReadInt();
        if (n == -1) return;
        if (n == 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine(0);
            return;
        }
        long stockLength = scanner.ReadLong();
        
        long[] lengths = new long[n];
        int[] indices = new int[n];
        
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            lengths[i] = scanner.ReadLong();
            indices[i] = i;
        }
        
        Array.Sort(lengths, indices, new DescendingComparer());
        
        long[] tree = new long[4 * n];
        Array.Fill(tree, stockLength);
        
        int[] assignedBin = new int[n];
        int maxBin = -1;
        
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            if (tree[1] < lengths[i])
            {
                throw new Exception("Item exceeds stock length or no bins available.");
            }
            int binIndex = QueryAndUpdate(1, 0, n - 1, lengths[i], tree);
            assignedBin[indices[i]] = binIndex;
            if (binIndex > maxBin)
            {
                maxBin = binIndex;
            }
        }
        
        int numStocks = maxBin + 1;
        
        int[] binCounts = new int[numStocks];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            binCounts[assignedBin[i]]++;
        }
        
        int[][] bins = new int[numStocks][];
        for (int i = 0; i < numStocks; i++)
        {
            bins[i] = new int[binCounts[i]];
        }
        
        int[] binPointers = new int[numStocks];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int b = assignedBin[i];
            bins[b][binPointers[b]++] = i;
        }
        
        using var outStream = Console.OpenStandardOutput();
        using var writer = new FastWriter(outStream);
        
        writer.Write(numStocks);
        writer.WriteLine();
        for (int i = 0; i < numStocks; i++)
        {
            var bin = bins[i];
            for (int j = 0; j < bin.Length; j++)
            {
                writer.Write(bin[j]);
                if (j < bin.Length - 1)
                {
                    writer.WriteSpace();
                }
            }
            writer.WriteLine();
        }
    }
    
    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    static int QueryAndUpdate(int node, int l, int r, long val, long[] tree)
    {
        if (l == r)
        {
            tree[node] -= val;
            return l;
        }
        int mid = l + (r - l) / 2;
        int res;
        if (tree[2 * node] >= val)
        {
            res = QueryAndUpdate(2 * node, l, mid, val, tree);
        }
        else
        {
            res = QueryAndUpdate(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, r, val, tree);
        }
        tree[node] = Math.Max(tree[2 * node], tree[2 * node + 1]);
        return res;
    }
}

readonly struct DescendingComparer : IComparer<long>
{
    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public int Compare(long x, long y)
    {
        return y.CompareTo(x);
    }
}

class FastScanner
{
    private Stream _stream;
    private byte[] _buffer = new byte[1024 * 1024];
    private int _head;
    private int _tail;

    public FastScanner(Stream stream)
    {
        _stream = stream;
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public int ReadInt()
    {
        int c = Read();
        while (c <= 32)
        {
            if (c == -1) return -1;
            c = Read();
        }
        int res = 0;
        while (c > 32)
        {
            res = res * 10 + c - '0';
            c = Read();
        }
        return res;
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public long ReadLong()
    {
        int c = Read();
        while (c <= 32)
        {
            if (c == -1) return -1;
            c = Read();
        }
        long res = 0;
        while (c > 32)
        {
            res = res * 10 + c - '0';
            c = Read();
        }
        return res;
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    private int Read()
    {
        if (_head >= _tail)
        {
            _head = 0;
            _tail = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
            if (_tail == 0) return -1;
        }
        return _buffer[_head++];
    }
}

class FastWriter : IDisposable
{
    private Stream _stream;
    private byte[] _buffer;
    private int _pos;

    public FastWriter(Stream stream, int bufferSize = 1024 * 1024)
    {
        _stream = stream;
        _buffer = new byte[bufferSize];
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void Write(int value)
    {
        if (value == 0)
        {
            WriteChar('0');
            return;
        }

        if (value < 0)
        {
            WriteChar('-');
            value = -value;
        }

        int temp = value;
        int numDigits = 0;
        while (temp > 0)
        {
            temp /= 10;
            numDigits++;
        }

        if (_pos + numDigits > _buffer.Length)
        {
            Flush();
        }

        int p = _pos + numDigits - 1;
        while (value > 0)
        {
            _buffer[p--] = (byte)('0' + (value % 10));
            value /= 10;
        }
        _pos += numDigits;
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteChar(char c)
    {
        if (_pos == _buffer.Length) Flush();
        _buffer[_pos++] = (byte)c;
    }
    
    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteLine() => WriteChar('\n');
    
    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteSpace() => WriteChar(' ');

    public void Flush()
    {
        if (_pos > 0)
        {
            _stream.Write(_buffer, 0, _pos);
            _pos = 0;
        }
    }

    public void Dispose()
    {
        Flush();
    }
}

Test 10: 2D Cutting Stock - Rectangle Packing (C++) Score: 12.10 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 2D Cutting Stock Problem (Rectangle Bin Packing) in C++.

Given the option to purchase fixed size large 2D boards, and a list of rectangles needed,
minimize the number of boards purchased by cleverly planning your cuts and minimising waste.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 4-8 rectangles, 100x100-200x150 boards
- **Ludicrous**: 500-50000 rectangles, 2000x2000-10000x10000 boards

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N board_width board_height
Next N lines: w h (rectangle dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_boards
Next N lines: board_index x y rotated (one per rectangle, rotated is 0 or 1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size and board dimensions
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for 50000 rectangles
3. **Quality**: Minimize number of boards used while producing valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Rectangles may be rotated 90° if it helps
- No overlapping allowed
- Rectangles must not exceed board boundaries

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35730 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a set of rectangles into as few fixed-size boards as possible, with no overlapping. Rectangles may be rotated 90° to fit better. Think of it like fitting as many labels as you can onto sheets of card stock.

This 2D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of rectangles. The baseline stacks rectangles in simple horizontal rows.

 1.0000
[4 rectangles, 100x100 board] Boards: 1, Baseline: 1, Waste: 4100, Time: 0.0s - excellent (≤ baseline). (waste < 1 board, no repack check)

2D Cutting Stock - 4 rectangles, 100x100 board

View Code (9233 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <chrono>

using namespace std;

const long long INF = 1e18;

chrono::time_point<chrono::steady_clock> start_time;

double elapsed() {
    auto now = chrono::steady_clock::now();
    return chrono::duration<double>(now - start_time).count();
}

struct FRect {
    int x, y, w, h;
};

struct Board {
    vector<FRect> free_rects;
    int max_dim1, max_dim2;

    Board(int w, int h) {
        free_rects.push_back({0, 0, w, h});
        update_stats();
    }

    void update_stats() {
        max_dim1 = 0;
        max_dim2 = 0;
        for (const auto& f : free_rects) {
            int fw = f.w;
            int fh = f.h;
            if (fw < fh) {
                if (fw > max_dim1) max_dim1 = fw;
                if (fh > max_dim2) max_dim2 = fh;
            } else {
                if (fh > max_dim1) max_dim1 = fh;
                if (fw > max_dim2) max_dim2 = fw;
            }
        }
    }
};

struct Placement {
    int board_index;
    int x, y;
    int rotated;
};

struct RectInput {
    int id;
    int w, h;
};

struct Score {
    long long score1;
    long long score2;
    bool operator<(const Score& o) const {
        if (score1 != o.score1) return score1 < o.score1;
        return score2 < o.score2;
    }
};

enum FitAlgo { BSSF, BAF, BLSF };

Score get_bssf(const FRect& F, int w, int h) {
    return { min(F.w - w, F.h - h), max(F.w - w, F.h - h) };
}

Score get_baf(const FRect& F, int w, int h) {
    return { 1LL * F.w * F.h - 1LL * w * h, (long long)min(F.w - w, F.h - h) };
}

Score get_blsf(const FRect& F, int w, int h) {
    return { max(F.w - w, F.h - h), min(F.w - w, F.h - h) };
}

bool split_rect(const FRect& F, int px, int py, int pw, int ph, vector<FRect>& new_rects) {
    if (px >= F.x + F.w || px + pw <= F.x || py >= F.y + F.h || py + ph <= F.y) {
        return false;
    }
    if (py > F.y) new_rects.push_back({F.x, F.y, F.w, py - F.y});
    if (py + ph < F.y + F.h) new_rects.push_back({F.x, py + ph, F.w, F.y + F.h - (py + ph)});
    if (px > F.x) new_rects.push_back({F.x, F.y, px - F.x, F.h});
    if (px + pw < F.x + F.w) new_rects.push_back({px + pw, F.y, F.x + F.w - (px + pw), F.h});
    return true;
}

void prune(vector<FRect>& rects, int max_free) {
    for (int i = 0; i < (int)rects.size(); ++i) {
        for (int j = 0; j < (int)rects.size(); ++j) {
            if (i == j) continue;
            if (rects[i].x >= rects[j].x && rects[i].y >= rects[j].y &&
                rects[i].x + rects[i].w <= rects[j].x + rects[j].w &&
                rects[i].y + rects[i].h <= rects[j].y + rects[j].h) {
                rects[i] = rects.back();
                rects.pop_back();
                --i;
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    if ((int)rects.size() > max_free) {
        sort(rects.begin(), rects.end(), [](const FRect& a, const FRect& b) {
            return 1LL * a.w * a.h > 1LL * b.w * b.h;
        });
        rects.resize(max_free);
    }
}

void place_rect(Board& b, int px, int py, int pw, int ph, int max_free) {
    int n = b.free_rects.size();
    vector<FRect> new_rects;
    new_rects.reserve(n * 4);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (split_rect(b.free_rects[i], px, py, pw, ph, new_rects)) {
            b.free_rects[i] = b.free_rects.back();
            b.free_rects.pop_back();
            --i;
            --n;
        }
    }
    b.free_rects.insert(b.free_rects.end(), new_rects.begin(), new_rects.end());
    prune(b.free_rects, max_free);
    b.update_stats();
}

vector<Placement> solve_heuristic(vector<RectInput> rects, int BOARD_W, int BOARD_H, int sort_algo, FitAlgo fit_algo, int max_free, bool is_first) {
    if (sort_algo == 0) {
        sort(rects.begin(), rects.end(), [](const RectInput& a, const RectInput& b) {
            long long a_area = 1LL * a.w * a.h;
            long long b_area = 1LL * b.w * b.h;
            if (a_area != b_area) return a_area > b_area;
            return max(a.w, a.h) > max(b.w, b.h);
        });
    } else if (sort_algo == 1) {
        sort(rects.begin(), rects.end(), [](const RectInput& a, const RectInput& b) {
            int a_max = max(a.w, a.h);
            int b_max = max(b.w, b.h);
            if (a_max != b_max) return a_max > b_max;
            return 1LL * a.w * a.h > 1LL * b.w * b.h;
        });
    } else if (sort_algo == 2) {
        sort(rects.begin(), rects.end(), [](const RectInput& a, const RectInput& b) {
            int a_per = a.w + a.h;
            int b_per = b.w + b.h;
            if (a_per != b_per) return a_per > b_per;
            return 1LL * a.w * a.h > 1LL * b.w * b.h;
        });
    }

    vector<Board> boards;
    boards.reserve(rects.size());
    vector<Placement> ans(rects.size());

    for (const auto& r : rects) {
        if (!is_first && elapsed() > 28.0) {
            return {}; 
        }

        int rw = min(r.w, r.h);
        int rh = max(r.w, r.h);

        int best_board = -1;
        int best_frect = -1;
        bool best_rot = false;
        Score best_score = {INF, INF};

        for (int b = 0; b < (int)boards.size(); ++b) {
            if (rw > boards[b].max_dim1 || rh > boards[b].max_dim2) continue;

            for (int f = 0; f < (int)boards[b].free_rects.size(); ++f) {
                const auto& F = boards[b].free_rects[f];
                
                if (r.w <= F.w && r.h <= F.h) {
                    Score s;
                    if (fit_algo == BSSF) s = get_bssf(F, r.w, r.h);
                    else if (fit_algo == BAF) s = get_baf(F, r.w, r.h);
                    else s = get_blsf(F, r.w, r.h);
                    
                    if (s < best_score) {
                        best_score = s; best_board = b; best_frect = f; best_rot = false;
                    }
                }
                
                if (r.h <= F.w && r.w <= F.h) {
                    Score s;
                    if (fit_algo == BSSF) s = get_bssf(F, r.h, r.w);
                    else if (fit_algo == BAF) s = get_baf(F, r.h, r.w);
                    else s = get_blsf(F, r.h, r.w);
                    
                    if (s < best_score) {
                        best_score = s; best_board = b; best_frect = f; best_rot = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if (best_board == -1) {
            boards.emplace_back(BOARD_W, BOARD_H);
            best_board = boards.size() - 1;
            best_frect = 0;
            
            Score s1 = {INF, INF}, s2 = {INF, INF};
            if (r.w <= BOARD_W && r.h <= BOARD_H) {
                if (fit_algo == BSSF) s1 = get_bssf(boards.back().free_rects[0], r.w, r.h);
                else if (fit_algo == BAF) s1 = get_baf(boards.back().free_rects[0], r.w, r.h);
                else s1 = get_blsf(boards.back().free_rects[0], r.w, r.h);
                best_rot = false;
            }
            if (r.h <= BOARD_W && r.w <= BOARD_H) {
                if (fit_algo == BSSF) s2 = get_bssf(boards.back().free_rects[0], r.h, r.w);
                else if (fit_algo == BAF) s2 = get_baf(boards.back().free_rects[0], r.h, r.w);
                else s2 = get_blsf(boards.back().free_rects[0], r.h, r.w);
                if (s2 < s1) best_rot = true;
            }
        }

        const auto& F = boards[best_board].free_rects[best_frect];
        int px = F.x;
        int py = F.y;
        int pw = best_rot ? r.h : r.w;
        int ph = best_rot ? r.w : r.h;

        ans[r.id] = {best_board, px, py, best_rot ? 1 : 0};
        
        place_rect(boards[best_board], px, py, pw, ph, max_free);
    }
    
    return ans;
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(NULL);

    int N, BOARD_W, BOARD_H;
    if (!(cin >> N >> BOARD_W >> BOARD_H)) return 0;

    vector<RectInput> rects(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        rects[i].id = i;
        cin >> rects[i].w >> rects[i].h;
    }

    start_time = chrono::steady_clock::now();

    int max_free = 400;
    if (N > 2000) max_free = 200;
    if (N > 10000) max_free = 100;
    if (N > 30000) max_free = 50;

    int best_total_boards = 2e9;
    vector<Placement> best_ans;

    vector<pair<int, FitAlgo>> heuristics = {
        {0, BAF}, {0, BSSF}, {0, BLSF},
        {1, BAF}, {1, BSSF}, {1, BLSF},
        {2, BAF}, {2, BSSF}, {2, BLSF}
    };

    bool is_first = true;
    for (const auto& heur : heuristics) {
        if (!is_first && elapsed() > 28.0) break;
        
        auto ans = solve_heuristic(rects, BOARD_W, BOARD_H, heur.first, heur.second, max_free, is_first);
        if (ans.empty()) continue; 

        int max_board = 0;
        for (const auto& p : ans) {
            if (p.board_index > max_board) max_board = p.board_index;
        }
        int total_boards = max_board + 1;

        if (total_boards < best_total_boards) {
            best_total_boards = total_boards;
            best_ans = ans;
        }
        is_first = false;
    }

    cout << best_total_boards << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        cout << best_ans[i].board_index << " " 
             << best_ans[i].x << " " 
             << best_ans[i].y << " " 
             << best_ans[i].rotated << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 11: 2D Polygon Cutting Stock (Rust) Score: 1.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 2D Polygon Cutting Stock Problem in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with problems ranging from 2 simple pieces to 5000+ complex polygons.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: SV NP (number of stock polygon vertices, number of pieces)
Next SV lines: x y (stock polygon vertices)
For each piece:
Line: PV (number of vertices in this piece)
Next PV lines: x y (piece polygon vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next NP lines: stock_index x y rotation (one per piece)
stock_index: 0-indexed stock polygon
x y: translation to apply
rotation: angle in radians

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and complexity of pieces
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 5000+ complex pieces
3. **Quality**: Minimize stock usage while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Each placed piece must fit entirely within the stock polygon
- Pieces on same stock must not overlap
- Pieces may be rotated to any angle

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Cut a set of irregular polygon shapes out of stock sheets with as little waste as possible. Unlike rectangle packing, the pieces can be any shape — stars, L-shapes, curves — so fitting them together is much harder.

The AI must handle polygon geometry (containment, intersection, rotation) and plan efficient layouts. Subpasses increase the number and complexity of the pieces. The baseline uses a simple greedy placement strategy.

 0.0000
[4 rectangles from 100x100 square] Invalid: Placement 1 overlaps with placement 0

Polygon Cutting - 4 rectangles from 100x100 square

View Code (19137 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use std::time::{Instant, Duration};

const TIME_LIMIT_SECS: f64 = 285.0; // 4 minutes 45 seconds to be safe

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Point { x: f64, y: f64 }

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
struct BoundingBox {
    min_x: f64, max_x: f64,
    min_y: f64, max_y: f64,
}

impl BoundingBox {
    fn from_points(points: &[Point]) -> Self {
        let mut min_x = f64::MAX; let mut max_x = f64::MIN;
        let mut min_y = f64::MAX; let mut max_y = f64::MIN;
        for p in points {
            if p.x < min_x { min_x = p.x; }
            if p.x > max_x { max_x = p.x; }
            if p.y < min_y { min_y = p.y; }
            if p.y > max_y { max_y = p.y; }
        }
        Self { min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y }
    }
    
    fn intersects(&self, other: &BoundingBox) -> bool {
        self.min_x <= other.max_x + 1e-6 &&
        self.max_x >= other.min_x - 1e-6 &&
        self.min_y <= other.max_y + 1e-6 &&
        self.max_y >= other.min_y - 1e-6
    }
    
    fn is_inside(&self, other: &BoundingBox) -> bool {
        self.min_x >= other.min_x - 1e-6 &&
        self.max_x <= other.max_x + 1e-6 &&
        self.min_y >= other.min_y - 1e-6 &&
        self.max_y <= other.max_y + 1e-6
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
struct Polygon {
    vertices: Vec<Point>,
    bb: BoundingBox,
}

impl Polygon {
    fn new(vertices: Vec<Point>) -> Self {
        let bb = BoundingBox::from_points(&vertices);
        Self { vertices, bb }
    }
    
    fn update_bounding_box(&mut self) {
        self.bb = BoundingBox::from_points(&self.vertices);
    }

    fn translate(&self, tx: f64, ty: f64) -> Self {
        let vertices = self.vertices.iter().map(|p| Point { x: p.x + tx, y: p.y + ty }).collect();
        let mut bb = self.bb.clone();
        bb.min_x += tx; bb.max_x += tx;
        bb.min_y += ty; bb.max_y += ty;
        Self { vertices, bb }
    }
}

fn calculate_area(vertices: &[Point]) -> f64 {
    let mut area = 0.0;
    let n = vertices.len();
    for i in 0..n {
        let j = (i + 1) % n;
        area += vertices[i].x * vertices[j].y;
        area -= vertices[j].x * vertices[i].y;
    }
    (area / 2.0).abs()
}

fn polygon_centroid(vertices: &[Point]) -> Point {
    let mut cx = 0.0;
    let mut cy = 0.0;
    let mut area = 0.0;
    let n = vertices.len();
    for i in 0..n {
        let j = (i + 1) % n;
        let cross = vertices[i].x * vertices[j].y - vertices[j].x * vertices[i].y;
        cx += (vertices[i].x + vertices[j].x) * cross;
        cy += (vertices[i].y + vertices[j].y) * cross;
        area += cross;
    }
    area *= 0.5;
    if area.abs() < 1e-9 {
        let mut sum_x = 0.0;
        let mut sum_y = 0.0;
        for p in vertices {
            sum_x += p.x;
            sum_y += p.y;
        }
        return Point { x: sum_x / n as f64, y: sum_y / n as f64 };
    }
    cx /= 6.0 * area;
    cy /= 6.0 * area;
    Point { x: cx, y: cy }
}

fn shrink_polygon(poly: &mut Polygon, factor: f64) {
    let c = polygon_centroid(&poly.vertices);
    for p in &mut poly.vertices {
        p.x = c.x + (p.x - c.x) * factor;
        p.y = c.y + (p.y - c.y) * factor;
    }
    poly.update_bounding_box();
}

fn rotate_polygon(points: &[Point], angle: f64) -> Vec<Point> {
    let cos_a = angle.cos();
    let sin_a = angle.sin();
    points.iter().map(|p| Point {
        x: p.x * cos_a - p.y * sin_a,
        y: p.x * sin_a + p.y * cos_a,
    }).collect()
}

fn cross_product(p1: Point, p2: Point, p3: Point) -> f64 {
    (p2.x - p1.x) * (p3.y - p1.y) - (p2.y - p1.y) * (p3.x - p1.x)
}

fn on_segment(p: Point, q: Point, r: Point) -> bool {
    q.x <= p.x.max(r.x) + 1e-9 && q.x >= p.x.min(r.x) - 1e-9 &&
    q.y <= p.y.max(r.y) + 1e-9 && q.y >= p.y.min(r.y) - 1e-9
}

fn segments_intersect(p1: Point, q1: Point, p2: Point, q2: Point) -> bool {
    let d1 = cross_product(p2, q2, p1);
    let d2 = cross_product(p2, q2, q1);
    let d3 = cross_product(p1, q1, p2);
    let d4 = cross_product(p1, q1, q2);

    if ((d1 > 1e-9 && d2 < -1e-9) || (d1 < -1e-9 && d2 > 1e-9)) &&
       ((d3 > 1e-9 && d4 < -1e-9) || (d3 < -1e-9 && d4 > 1e-9)) {
        return true;
    }

    if d1.abs() <= 1e-9 && on_segment(p2, p1, q2) { return true; }
    if d2.abs() <= 1e-9 && on_segment(p2, q1, q2) { return true; }
    if d3.abs() <= 1e-9 && on_segment(p1, p2, q1) { return true; }
    if d4.abs() <= 1e-9 && on_segment(p1, q2, q1) { return true; }

    false
}

fn is_point_inside_polygon(p: Point, poly: &Polygon) -> bool {
    let mut inside = false;
    let mut j = poly.vertices.len() - 1;
    for i in 0..poly.vertices.len() {
        let vi = poly.vertices[i];
        let vj = poly.vertices[j];
        
        let intersect = ((vi.y > p.y) != (vj.y > p.y)) &&
                        (p.x < (vj.x - vi.x) * (p.y - vi.y) / (vj.y - vi.y) + vi.x);
        if intersect {
            inside = !inside;
        }
        j = i;
    }
    inside
}

fn is_polygon_inside(piece: &Polygon, stock: &Polygon) -> bool {
    if !piece.bb.is_inside(&stock.bb) {
        return false;
    }
    
    for i in 0..piece.vertices.len() {
        let e1_1 = piece.vertices[i];
        let e1_2 = piece.vertices[(i+1)%piece.vertices.len()];
        for j in 0..stock.vertices.len() {
            let e2_1 = stock.vertices[j];
            let e2_2 = stock.vertices[(j+1)%stock.vertices.len()];
            if segments_intersect(e1_1, e1_2, e2_1, e2_2) {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }
    
    if !is_point_inside_polygon(piece.vertices[0], stock) {
        return false;
    }
    true
}

fn do_polygons_intersect(p1: &Polygon, p2: &Polygon) -> bool {
    if !p1.bb.intersects(&p2.bb) {
        return false;
    }
    for i in 0..p1.vertices.len() {
        let e1_1 = p1.vertices[i];
        let e1_2 = p1.vertices[(i+1)%p1.vertices.len()];
        for j in 0..p2.vertices.len() {
            let e2_1 = p2.vertices[j];
            let e2_2 = p2.vertices[(j+1)%p2.vertices.len()];
            if segments_intersect(e1_1, e1_2, e2_1, e2_2) {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }
    if is_point_inside_polygon(p1.vertices[0], p2) { return true; }
    if is_point_inside_polygon(p2.vertices[0], p1) { return true; }
    false
}

struct SpatialGrid {
    min_x: f64, min_y: f64,
    cell_w: f64, cell_h: f64,
    cols: usize, rows: usize,
    cells: Vec<Vec<usize>>,
}

impl SpatialGrid {
    fn new(bb: &BoundingBox, cols: usize, rows: usize) -> Self {
        let mut cell_w = (bb.max_x - bb.min_x) / cols as f64;
        let mut cell_h = (bb.max_y - bb.min_y) / rows as f64;
        if cell_w < 1e-6 { cell_w = 1.0; }
        if cell_h < 1e-6 { cell_h = 1.0; }
        Self {
            min_x: bb.min_x,
            min_y: bb.min_y,
            cell_w,
            cell_h,
            cols,
            rows,
            cells: vec![vec![]; cols * rows],
        }
    }
    
    fn insert(&mut self, piece_idx: usize, bb: &BoundingBox) {
        let start_col = ((bb.min_x - self.min_x) / self.cell_w).floor() as isize;
        let end_col = ((bb.max_x - self.min_x) / self.cell_w).ceil() as isize;
        let start_row = ((bb.min_y - self.min_y) / self.cell_h).floor() as isize;
        let end_row = ((bb.max_y - self.min_y) / self.cell_h).ceil() as isize;

        let start_col = start_col.clamp(0, self.cols as isize - 1) as usize;
        let end_col = end_col.clamp(0, self.cols as isize - 1) as usize;
        let start_row = start_row.clamp(0, self.rows as isize - 1) as usize;
        let end_row = end_row.clamp(0, self.rows as isize - 1) as usize;

        for r in start_row..=end_row {
            for c in start_col..=end_col {
                self.cells[r * self.cols + c].push(piece_idx);
            }
        }
    }
    
    fn get_candidates(&self, bb: &BoundingBox, res: &mut Vec<usize>) {
        res.clear();
        let start_col = ((bb.min_x - self.min_x) / self.cell_w).floor() as isize;
        let end_col = ((bb.max_x - self.min_x) / self.cell_w).ceil() as isize;
        let start_row = ((bb.min_y - self.min_y) / self.cell_h).floor() as isize;
        let end_row = ((bb.max_y - self.min_y) / self.cell_h).ceil() as isize;

        let start_col = start_col.clamp(0, self.cols as isize - 1) as usize;
        let end_col = end_col.clamp(0, self.cols as isize - 1) as usize;
        let start_row = start_row.clamp(0, self.rows as isize - 1) as usize;
        let end_row = end_row.clamp(0, self.rows as isize - 1) as usize;

        for r in start_row..=end_row {
            for c in start_col..=end_col {
                res.extend(&self.cells[r * self.cols + c]);
            }
        }
        res.sort_unstable();
        res.dedup();
    }
}

struct PlacedPiece {
    id: usize,
    poly: Polygon,
    tx: f64, ty: f64, rot: f64,
}

struct StockState {
    placed_pieces: Vec<PlacedPiece>,
    grid: SpatialGrid,
    stock_bb: BoundingBox,
    placed_area: f64,
}

impl StockState {
    fn new(stock_bb: BoundingBox) -> Self {
        Self {
            placed_pieces: Vec::new(),
            grid: SpatialGrid::new(&stock_bb, 20, 20),
            stock_bb,
            placed_area: 0.0,
        }
    }
    
    fn add_piece(&mut self, id: usize, poly: Polygon, tx: f64, ty: f64, rot: f64, area: f64) {
        self.grid.insert(self.placed_pieces.len(), &poly.bb);
        self.placed_pieces.push(PlacedPiece { id, poly, tx, ty, rot });
        self.placed_area += area;
    }
    
    fn is_valid_placement(&self, piece: &Polygon, stock_poly: &Polygon, candidates_buf: &mut Vec<usize>) -> bool {
        if !is_polygon_inside(piece, stock_poly) {
            return false;
        }
        
        self.grid.get_candidates(&piece.bb, candidates_buf);
        for &idx in candidates_buf.iter() {
            if do_polygons_intersect(piece, &self.placed_pieces[idx].poly) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        true
    }
}

struct Piece {
    id: usize,
    vertices: Vec<Point>,
    area: f64,
}

struct RotatedPiece {
    rot: f64,
    shrunk_poly: Polygon,
    original_poly: Polygon,
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines().map(|l| l.unwrap());
    
    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap();
    let mut parts = first_line.split_whitespace();
    let sv: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let np: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    
    let mut stock_vertices = Vec::with_capacity(sv);
    for _ in 0..sv {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap();
        let mut parts = line.split_whitespace();
        let x: f64 = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        stock_vertices.push(Point { x, y });
    }
    
    let base_stock_poly = Polygon::new(stock_vertices);
    
    let mut pieces = Vec::with_capacity(np);
    for i in 0..np {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap();
        let pv: usize = line.trim().parse().unwrap();
        let mut vertices = Vec::with_capacity(pv);
        for _ in 0..pv {
            let line = lines.next().unwrap();
            let mut parts = line.split_whitespace();
            let x: f64 = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            let y: f64 = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            vertices.push(Point { x, y });
        }
        let area = calculate_area(&vertices);
        pieces.push(Piece { id: i, vertices, area });
    }
    
    pieces.sort_by(|a, b| b.area.partial_cmp(&a.area).unwrap());
    
    let mut stocks: Vec<StockState> = Vec::new();
    let start_time = Instant::now();
    let rotations = [0.0, std::f64::consts::PI / 2.0, std::f64::consts::PI, 3.0 * std::f64::consts::PI / 2.0];
    
    let mut candidates_buf = Vec::new();
    
    for (piece_idx, piece) in pieces.iter().enumerate() {
        let elapsed = start_time.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
        let remaining_time = (TIME_LIMIT_SECS - elapsed).max(0.01);
        let remaining_pieces = (np - piece_idx) as f64;
        let time_for_this_piece = remaining_time / remaining_pieces;
        
        let piece_start = Instant::now();
        
        let mut rotated_pieces = Vec::new();
        for rot in rotations.iter() {
            let original_poly = Polygon::new(rotate_polygon(&piece.vertices, *rot));
            let mut shrunk_poly = original_poly.clone();
            shrink_polygon(&mut shrunk_poly, 0.99999);
            rotated_pieces.push(RotatedPiece {
                rot: *rot,
                shrunk_poly,
                original_poly,
            });
        }
        
        let mut stock_order: Vec<usize> = (0..stocks.len()).collect();
        stock_order.sort_by(|&a, &b| stocks[b].placed_area.partial_cmp(&stocks[a].placed_area).unwrap());
        stock_order.push(stocks.len()); // Option for new stock
        
        let mut best_global_placement = None;
        let mut best_global_score = f64::MAX;
        
        let grid_size = 40;
        
        'stock_loop: for &stock_idx in &stock_order {
            let is_new_stock = stock_idx == stocks.len();
            let penalty = if is_new_stock { 1000000.0 } else { 0.0 };
            let stock_bb = if is_new_stock { &base_stock_poly.bb } else { &stocks[stock_idx].stock_bb };
            
            for rp in &rotated_pieces {
                let min_tx = stock_bb.min_x - rp.original_poly.bb.min_x;
                let max_tx = stock_bb.max_x - rp.original_poly.bb.max_x;
                let min_ty = stock_bb.min_y - rp.original_poly.bb.min_y;
                let max_ty = stock_bb.max_y - rp.original_poly.bb.max_y;
                
                if min_tx > max_tx || min_ty > max_ty {
                    continue;
                }
                
                let mut candidates = Vec::with_capacity((grid_size + 1) * (grid_size + 1));
                for y_step in 0..=grid_size {
                    let ty = min_ty + (max_ty - min_ty) * (y_step as f64 / grid_size as f64);
                    for x_step in 0..=grid_size {
                        let tx = min_tx + (max_tx - min_tx) * (x_step as f64 / grid_size as f64);
                        let score = ty + tx * 0.1;
                        candidates.push((score, tx, ty));
                    }
                }
                candidates.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.partial_cmp(&b.0).unwrap());
                
                let mut loops = 0;
                for (score, tx, ty) in candidates {
                    loops += 1;
                    if loops % 100 == 0 {
                        if piece_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() > time_for_this_piece {
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    
                    let total_score = score + penalty;
                    if total_score >= best_global_score {
                        break; 
                    }
                    
                    let test_poly = rp.shrunk_poly.translate(tx, ty);
                    let valid = if is_new_stock {
                        is_polygon_inside(&test_poly, &base_stock_poly)
                    } else {
                        stocks[stock_idx].is_valid_placement(&test_poly, &base_stock_poly, &mut candidates_buf)
                    };
                    
                    if valid {
                        best_global_score = total_score;
                        best_global_placement = Some((stock_idx, tx, ty, rp.rot, rp.original_poly.translate(tx, ty)));
                        break; 
                    }
                }
            }
            
            if is_new_stock && best_global_placement.is_some() {
                break;
            }
        }
        
        if best_global_placement.is_none() {
            let mut rng_seed = 12345 + piece.id as u32;
            fn next_rand(seed: &mut u32) -> f64 {
                *seed = seed.wrapping_mul(1664525).wrapping_add(1013904223);
                (*seed as f64) / (u32::MAX as f64)
            }
        
            let mut attempts = 0;
            'fallback: loop {
                attempts += 1;
                let stock_idx = if next_rand(&mut rng_seed) < 0.2 && !stocks.is_empty() {
                    (next_rand(&mut rng_seed) * stocks.len() as f64) as usize
                } else {
                    stocks.len()
                };
                
                let is_new_stock = stock_idx == stocks.len();
                let stock_bb = if is_new_stock { &base_stock_poly.bb } else { &stocks[stock_idx].stock_bb };
                
                let rp_idx = (next_rand(&mut rng_seed) * rotated_pieces.len() as f64) as usize;
                let rp = &rotated_pieces[rp_idx];
                
                let min_tx = stock_bb.min_x - rp.original_poly.bb.min_x;
                let max_tx = stock_bb.max_x - rp.original_poly.bb.max_x;
                let min_ty = stock_bb.min_y - rp.original_poly.bb.min_y;
                let max_ty = stock_bb.max_y - rp.original_poly.bb.max_y;
                
                if min_tx > max_tx || min_ty > max_ty {
                    if attempts > 10000 { break; }
                    continue;
                }
                
                let tx = min_tx + next_rand(&mut rng_seed) * (max_tx - min_tx);
                let ty = min_ty + next_rand(&mut rng_seed) * (max_ty - min_ty);
                
                let test_poly = rp.shrunk_poly.translate(tx, ty);
                
                let valid = if is_new_stock {
                    is_polygon_inside(&test_poly, &base_stock_poly)
                } else {
                    stocks[stock_idx].is_valid_placement(&test_poly, &base_stock_poly, &mut candidates_buf)
                };
                
                if valid {
                    best_global_placement = Some((stock_idx, tx, ty, rp.rot, rp.original_poly.translate(tx, ty)));
                    break 'fallback;
                }
                
                if attempts > 50000 {
                    best_global_placement = Some((stocks.len(), 0.0, 0.0, rp.rot, rp.original_poly.translate(0.0, 0.0)));
                    break 'fallback;
                }
            }
        }
        
        if let Some((stock_idx, tx, ty, rot, final_poly)) = best_global_placement {
            if stock_idx == stocks.len() {
                let mut new_stock = StockState::new(base_stock_poly.bb.clone());
                new_stock.add_piece(piece.id, final_poly, tx, ty, rot, piece.area);
                stocks.push(new_stock);
            } else {
                stocks[stock_idx].add_piece(piece.id, final_poly, tx, ty, rot, piece.area);
            }
        }
    }
    
    println!("{}", stocks.len());
    let mut output = vec![None; np];
    for (stock_idx, stock) in stocks.iter().enumerate() {
        for placed in &stock.placed_pieces {
            output[placed.id] = Some((stock_idx, placed.tx, placed.ty, placed.rot));
        }
    }
    
    for i in 0..np {
        if let Some((s, x, y, r)) = output[i] {
            println!("{} {} {} {}", s, x, y, r);
        } else {
            println!("0 0.0 0.0 0.0");
        }
    }
}
Layout
